title: Customer Delivery Driver (Permanent 30 - 35 Hours) - Balbriggan
description: **About the role** At Tesco, we see the value in you –and right now we are hiring Customer Delivery Drivers to join our team Our core purpose is to serve Irish shoppers a little better every day and being a **Customer Delivery Driver** you are the first face our customers see. Being a Customer Delivery Driver is hands on, physical and full of variety. No two shifts are ever the same – customer’s look for help in all kinds of different ways. For most of the day you’ll feel like your own boss, delivering to your customers but with a team of managers and colleagues back at base ready to support you when you need them. **Shift Pattern:** Various shifts available **You will be responsible for** * Providing an excellent home shopping experience by putting our customers at the heart of everything we do is our main responsibility. * Loading the van where required, and check your van for roadworthines * Understanding the schedule and route that has been calculated for you * Looking the part in your uniform, ready to help our customers * Driving safely, responsibly and within the law * To be courteous to all road users * Serving your customer with a smile and greet them by name * Taking care of your customer’s deliveries as if they were your own **You will need** * To have had a full IRISH or EU driving licence (manual or automatic) for at least 2 years with no more than 3 penalty points * To be a safe and courteous drivers * To enjoy helping others * To be personable with a good sense of humour and excellent communication skills * The ability to take the initiative and use common sense * To care about making a difference for customers and colleagues * The ability to stay calm under pressure Please be aware that as part of our recruitment process, job offers are subject to the Garda Vetting Process and you will need to provide proof of current penalty points on your licence at Assessment Stage. This can be requested from the National Driver Licence Services (NDLS) on **1890 41 61 41\. Press Option 4 and quote your driver number.** We are sorry but if you have been disqualified from driving within the last 10 years, we will not be able to accept your application. **Should you be successful in your application, your offer will be subject to and conditional upon you providing your bank account details before your agreed start date.** **Whats in it for you?** We offer excellent benefits that help make Tesco a great place to work. These include but aren’t limited to: * A variety of shifts * Part time and full time contracts available between 20 \- 39 hours a week * An hourly rate of pay for new drivers of €18\.24 per hour with additional premium for Sunday working * Annual Bonus Scheme * Colleague Clubcard offering 10% discount across stores and online, with 15% discount weekends once a month (Friday to Sunday), and 20% off twice a year. There is also 20% off F\&F clothing, all year long, these various discounts can be extended to a family member with a 2 nd Clubcard available.\* * Our Virtual GP service provides free and confidential access to an online GP through video GP consultations, and online prescription service for colleagues. The service is also available to families of colleagues who live at the same address for a reduced cost. * 24/7 access to our Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) for you and family members for in the moment emotional support along with free access to a range of wellbeing services supporting you under our new Wellbeing First approach of Body, Mind and Life. * Life Assurance * Pension Scheme\* * Save As You Earn Scheme\* * Paid Family Leave * Bike to Work * Long Service Awards * Commuter Scheme \*Entry requirements and rules of the scheme apply **About Us** Our vision at Tesco is to become every customer’s favourite way to shop, whether they are at home or out on the move. Our core purpose is “Serving our customers, communities and planet a little better every day”. Serving means more than a transactional relationship with our customers. It means acting as a responsible and sustainable business for all stakeholders, for the communities we are part of, and for the planet. Through initiatives like the Tesco Community Fund, our association with Children’s Health Foundation, our partnership with FoodCloud and our Stronger Starts programme, we have supported over 21,000 community groups, raised over €7\.5 million for medical equipment, and provided almost 15 million meals nationwide, to those living in food insecurity. Tesco has operated in the Irish retail market since 1997, and with 170 stores nationwide, we employ over 13,000 people in cities and towns around Ireland, supporting almost 45,000 jobs directly and indirectly. We partner with over 500 Irish suppliers \- of which almost three\-quarters are small and medium enterprises \- which in turn supports almost 13,000 farming families around Ireland. Tesco is the single largest buyer of Irish food and drink in the world, buying €1\.6 Billion a year; more than any other single country in the European Union, more than even the USA which you perceive as a massive buyer of Irish food. We work closely with suppliers across Ireland who are dedicated to making the best products for our customers and this is reflected in the numerous awards our products have won over the years including Blás Na hÉireann, World Steak Challenge and Great Taste Awards. We are proud to have an inclusive culture at Tesco where everyone truly feels able to be themselves. It is embedded in our values: we treat people how they want to be treated. At Tesco, we not only celebrate diversity, but recognise the value and opportunity it brings. We always want our colleagues to feel they can be themselves at work and we are committed to helping them be at their best. We know life looks a little different for each of us. That’s why at Tesco, we always welcome chats about flexible working. Some people are at the start of their careers, some want the freedom to do the things they love. Others are going through life\-changing moments like becoming a carer, nearing retirement, adapting to parenthood, or something else. So, talk to us throughout your application about how we can support. Tesco is committed to celebrating diversity and everyone is welcome at Tesco. We're committed to providing a fully inclusive recruitment process, allowing candidates the opportunity to thrive and inform us of any reasonable adjustments they may require. For further information on the accessibility support we can offer, please click http://roi.tesco\-careers.com/accessibility/ **How can I let Tesco know I need additional support?** Please let us know you need additional support by emailing **tescoireland.recruitment@tesco.ie** This will allow us to understand more about you and how we can best support you through the recruitment process. \#Tescodriver

title: Customer Service Representative
description: **About The Opportunity** Job Type: Permanent Application Deadline: 27 June 2026 **Job Description** **Title: Customer Service Representative** **Department: Client Services** **Location: City Centre Dublin, Ireland** **Salary: €32,100 plus bonus** Are you ready to make a real impact? Join Fidelity International as a Customer Service Representative in our modern Dublin city centre office and help our customers solve problems no one else can. Whether you're starting your career or taking the next step, you'll find an environment built for your success with expert training, ongoing support, and a strong sense of purpose. **About Fidelity International** With over 50 years’ experience, Fidelity helps millions worldwide build better financial futures across 25\+ locations. People are at the core of our business and we support each other and take pride in doing what’s right. **Inside the Client Services team** Joining our Client Services contact centre team means becoming part of a supportive community delivering expert client support. From day one, you’ll receive comprehensive training including classroom, digital tools, and hands\-on coaching to help you grow and succeed. **What you'll do:** Your role and responsibilities include: * Helping customers by handling queries with professionalism and empathy across multiple channels (primarily phone, with email and letters). * Explaining financial products clearly and guiding customers to helpful tools and information. * Taking ownership of each interaction, ensuring full resolution. * Logging and escalating any potential risks, fraud cases, or vulnerable situations. * Handling complaints constructively, aiming for early resolution. **What you'll bring:** * Experience in a customer service role, ideally in a corporate environment. * Excellent phone manner and verbal communication skills. * An appetite to grow your expertise through continued learning. * Strong listening skills and a customer\-first attitude. * Comfortable using digital tools and eager to learn new systems. * A team\-oriented approach and a genuine interest in helping others. **Why join us in Dublin?** We’re based in George’s Quay House, just steps from Grafton Street, the GPO, and Trinity College. We offer modern, flexible workspaces designed for both focus and connection, as well as an on\-site Barista Bar with coffee, fresh pastries, and made\-to\-order meals. **A role that offers real rewards:** We offer long\-term career stability, a clear development path, and a comprehensive benefits package including: * Pension contributions: 10% employer\-funded plus up to 3% matched * Private Medical Insurance: comprehensive cover for you and your family * Cycle to Work \& TaxSaver tickets: save on your commute * Gym discounts \& wellness support: prioritise your health * Enhanced parental \& family leave: support when it matters most * 24/7 Employee Assistance Programme: confidential mental health and wellbeing services * Dynamic working options: hybrid flexibility for home and office balance **Your future starts here:** If you’re ready to join a company that offers opportunity, growth, and purpose, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now.

title: Customer Support Agent
description: **The Company** At AccountsIQ, part of the AIQ Group, we’re on a mission to deeply understand our customers and deliver technology that enriches their lives. Alongside ExpenseIn, a leading expense management platform within the group, we provide smart, feature\-rich financial management software designed for ambitious businesses that have outgrown basic starter systems or seek a simpler, more affordable alternative to complex, costly ERP solutions. Our cloud\-based accounting software transforms the finance function, making it easier for multi\-entity businesses to capture, process, and report their financial data with ease and efficiency. **The Role** Reporting to the Support Manager, the successful candidate will work with the team to manage Support queries via ticket requests. The Support Agent will be customer\-focused, organised, a clear communicator, a keen trouble\-shooter and team player. **What you'll be doing:** * Answering tickets from customers, both internal and external, via Salesforce in a timely and professional manner. * Responding to customer queries who are users on our accounts software, ranging from user logins to report mismatches. Ensuring a resolution is achieved where possible in the first response. * Obtaining and evaluating all relevant data to handle queries, which may require contacting the user via phone. * Providing customers with the relevant information about the organisation’s service and product information. * Identifying, escalating priority issues and reporting to the senior team members. * Following up on complicated customer contacts, if needed, such as technical fixes. * Communicating and coordinating with internal departments as needed to resolve customer issues. * Recording details of comments, inquiries, complaints, and actions taken. Completing call notes when needed, and inputting this information into Salesforce. * Required to maintain and improve quality results by adhering to standards and guidelines; recommending improved procedures when you see fit. * Required to maintain industry knowledge by studying new product updates, such as new features or improved modules, and participating in educational opportunities. **What you'll need:** * 2\+ years’ experience in SaaS customer support, ideally in a high\-volume, ticket\-based environment * Experience using ticketing systems (e.g. Salesforce, Zendesk) to manage and resolve customer queries efficiently * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to investigate issues and identify root causes * Confident written and verbal communication skills, particularly when explaining financial or technical concepts to non\-expert users * Experience handling customer queries across multiple channels, including phone and email * High attention to detail and accuracy when working with customer data and financial information * Ability to manage multiple queries, prioritise effectively, and meet response SLAs * Proactive, ownership\-driven approach to resolving issues and improving the customer experience * Bookkeeping or accounting knowledge/experience is a strong advantage – solid understanding of reconciliations, journals, ledgers, and reporting discrepancies, with the ability to interpret and explain financial data confidently **What's the hiring process?** 1. An initial call with our People team to discuss your application. 2. An interview with the hiring manager. 3. A final interview with the hiring manager and our Head of Support, this may also include a small task/exercise which you'll be briefed on before the call. **Why work with us?** AIQ Group is a multi\-product SaaS business, bringing together ExpenseIn and AccountsIQ, and recently ranked \#477 in the Financial Times FT1000 list of Europe’s fastest\-growing companies in 2026\. As a forward\-thinking and collaborative organisation, we combine drive, energy and ambition with a supportive, down\-to\-earth culture that enables people to do their best work. Our TEAM values (Together, Energy, Action, and Mindset) sit at the core of how we work and grow as a business. Following a €60M investment in 2024, it’s an exciting time to join the group as we continue to scale, enhance our products, and elevate the service we provide to our customers. **Some of our perks** * 28 days annual leave \+ bank holidays \+ your birthday off * Hybrid working from our Dublin offices, or remote working within the United Kingdom * Working from abroad \- up to 6 weeks per calendar year * Private Health Insurance * 5% Pension contribution * Life Assurance \- 4 x Salary * Income Benefit * Wellbeing initiatives By submitting your application, you agree that AccountsIQ Group may collect your personal data for recruiting, global organisation planning, and related purposes. AccountsIQ Group’s Privacy Notice explains what personal information we may process, where we may process your personal information, our purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over AccountsIQ Group’s use of your personal information.

title: Data Analyst
description: **Data Analyst \- Financial Systems \& Investment Operations** **• Global investment management environment** **• SQL, reporting, and business systems focus** **• Opportunity to drive automation and process improvement** An exciting opportunity has opened for a Data Analyst to join a leading global investment management organisation. This role combines data analysis, business systems support, reporting, and process improvement, offering strong exposure to private markets and investment operations. You will become a key point of contact for Investran and related investment systems, supporting data management, reporting, and operational processes across the business. You'll work closely with stakeholders in Dublin and Switzerland on system enhancements, data quality initiatives, automation projects, and broader business improvement programs to increase efficiency and support continued growth. **Why This Role Stands Out** • Exposure to private equity and alternative investment operations • Opportunity to become a key systems and reporting expert within the business • Work on business\-critical data, reporting, and automation initiatives • Collaborate with international operations, technology, and business teams • Strong long\-term career development opportunities within a global organisation **What You'll Be Doing** • Support and enhance Investran and related investment management systems • Develop and maintain reporting solutions using SQL and business data tools • Analyse operational and investment data to support business decision\-making • Monitor and improve data quality, accuracy, and consistency across systems • Gather business requirements and support system enhancement projects • Assist with application support, testing, documentation, and system improvements • Identify opportunities for process optimisation, automation, and efficiency gains • Work closely with stakeholders to ensure systems and reporting meet business needs **What You Bring** • Experience in a Data Analyst, Reporting Analyst, Financial Systems Analyst, or similar role • Strong SQL and reporting experience • Experience supporting financial systems, investment platforms, or fund administration **Package \& Benefits** • Up to €55,000 \+ Pension contribution • Exposure to international investment operations and technology projects Interested in a role where you can combine data analysis, reporting, and financial systems expertise within a global investment environment? Contact Serena Akbib on \+353 1 960 9972 or send your CV to serena.akbib@archer.ie.

title: Data Center Operations Integration Technician
description: **Job Title: Data Centre Operations Integration Technician** **Location: Clondalkin** **Work Times: Monday to Friday (8am – 5pm)** **PURPOSE OF THE JOB** To manage risk and build technical knowledge during the handover of construction builds \& be the operational integration between the site operations \& the construction/CX team. The Operations Integration Tech will report to the Operational Integration supervisor, and the team will ensure that all construction activities are handed over in such a way that mitigates and minimises risk to the operational Site Operations Team Summarised below are the main tasks that we anticipate would fall within the remit of this position. **Key Responsibilities** * Work as part of the Operations Integration Team and ensure successful integration of new buildings on Campus to the Site Operations team. * Develop an in\-depth knowledge of the technical systems * Generate operational documentation to allow the Site Operations Team to operate and maintain the equipment safely and effectively. * Interface Works with existing Operations * Audit and challenge subcontractor works in the operational environment * Adherence to all quality, health \& safety, and environmental policies within the Data Centre domain * Ensure the development of site related procedures including EOPs, SOPs, MOPs, DRILLS as part of the facility handover to operations. All documentation should be consistent with current client standards. * Develop switching schedules in conjunction with the Technical Supervisors where shutdowns are required * Business / Ops Risk Assessments and Control Measures * Health and Safety Risk Assessments and Control Measures * Process and Approve CMOPs / Method Statements submitted * Attendance on Shutdowns and isolations during construction/commissioning as required * Generation of technical reports detailing the activities and issues relevant to the Transition Team for the Client \& operations as required. * Report health and safety breaches through the ‘HAZARD’ process * Ensure the professional image of CBRE is presented to clients and visitors at all times. * Develop a good working relationship with all members of CBRE staff and the Client representatives. * Ensure the provision of a Safe and Healthy working environment. To include the wearing of the CBRE uniform at all times and appropriate PPE as required. **For all new Builds (DUB’s)** * Act as “operational advocate” for start\-up and commissioning activities. * Attendance at Cx/Start\-up Meetings * Attendance at Integration Meetings * Attendance on Commissioning / walk downs * Attendance of SLT/IST, \& provide feedback on issues identified * O\&M Reviews and Sign off * Assist in the coordination of Vendor Training Programme for Technical \& Operations Shift team * Snagging / Defect Reporting * Warranty Management (in conjunction with the client) * Critical Spares – Assess requirements for new builds * Development of detailed emergency, maintenance, and drill procedures. **Qualifications, Experience and Character:** * A fully verifiable trade qualification in a relevant engineering discipline and verifiable additional training that is relevant to the proposed environment. * Experience at Technician level in a critical environment (preference is given to data center, hospital, pharmaceutical production or power plant experience). * Capability to manage escalations and emergency issues which contain various degrees of ambiguity and either resolve or properly escalate in a timely manner, where appropriate * An excellent understanding and experience of the electrical and mechanical systems used in a data center environment, including: Feeders, Transformers, Generators, Fuel Systems, Switchgear, UPS systems, Battery banks, ATS/STS units, PDU/PMM units, Chillers, Air handling units, CRAC units, Fire Alarm systems, and Fire Suppression systems. * Experience of complex automatic control equipment, including relay logic, programmable logic controllers (PLC’s), building management systems, and their integration with the data center infrastructure * Experience of medium voltage distribution systems and associated switch gear and protection equipment * Previous experience of working within a Project Environment and dealing with design and construction engineering professionals * Strong oral and written communication skills, with the ability to communicate intelligently and effectively with the client and with contractors who perform maintenance or upgrade work on the data center systems * Computer literate with the ability to compile and submit monthly and annual reports, and to monitor and trend operational characteristics (load, capacity, environmental conditions etc.). * Good working ability on Microsoft Office Products, in particular Excel, Word, Outlook and PowerPoint. * The ability to learn quickly and to gain maximum benefit from extensive training and development programs aimed at ensuring that any identified knowledge or experience deficiencies are addressed in the short term * Organised, able to effectively prioritise and deliver within a dynamic and high pressure, business critical environments * Self\-motivated, with ability to work on own initiative * Must have a high level of energy, be self\- starters, confident and stable in manner * Must understand what a Data Centre environment is, and the working restrictions applicable to such environments. * Must be able to demonstrate a career showing continuous personal development with at least two years’ experience in production or critical environments Please note All details are provided for guidance only, and do not necessarily limit the responsibilities and accountabilities of the job. Full details of employment terms and conditions are provided within Offers of Employment, The Employee Handbook and appropriate policies within the Company. **Why CBRE** When you join CBRE, you become part of the global leader in commercial real estate services and investment that helps businesses and people thrive. We are dynamic problem solvers and forward\-thinking professionals who create significant impact. Our collaborative culture is built on our shared values — respect, integrity, service and excellence — and we value the diverse perspectives, backgrounds and skillsets of our people. At CBRE, you have the opportunity to chart your own course and realize your potential. We welcome all applicants. **Applicant AI Use Disclosure** We value human interaction to understand each candidate's unique experience, skills and aspirations. We do not use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to make hiring decisions, and we ask that candidates disclose any use of AI in the application and interview process.

title: Data Quality Analyst
description: **About us:** Amach is an industry\-leading technology driven company with headquarters located in Dublin and remote teams in UK and Europe. Our blended teams of local and nearshore talent are optimised to deliver high quality and collaborative solutions. Founded in 2013, Amach was created to solve a specific problem in aviation: too much complexity, too little usable intelligence. We help airlines modernise their operating model using cloud, data and Al\-delivered by teams with deep aviation domain expertise. Our goal is to maximize airlines' operational efficiency by optimizing resource use, reduce costs and increase customer experience and satisfaction. We are seeking a Data Quality Analyst to join a growing Data \& AI Governance team. This is a hands\-on role focused on improving the quality, trust, and accountability of data across the organisation. You will work closely with business stakeholders, data owners, and technology teams to identify data quality issues, perform root cause analysis, support remediation activities, and help drive the adoption of data governance practices. **Location:** Hybrid (2 days per week in Dublin office) **Key responsibilities \& duties include:** * Investigate and resolve data quality issues. * Define and monitor data quality rules and KPIs. * Gather and document business requirements. * Support the adoption of data governance processes and data catalogues. * Collaborate with business and technical stakeholders to improve data quality. * Produce documentation, reporting, and analysis to support governance initiatives. **Required Experience:** * 3\+ years' experience in Data Quality, Data Governance, Data Analysis, or a related field. * Strong SQL and data analysis skills. * Experience working with data quality management and governance programmes. * Strong stakeholder management and communication skills. * Experience creating business and technical documentation. **Desirable:** * Experience with data quality and observability tools such as Monte Carlo, Soda, or Informatica. * Experience with data catalogues such as Atlan, Collibra, or Purview. * Experience with Power BI, Snowflake, Databricks, Jira, and Confluence. **What’s in it for you:** * An opportunity to join a fast\-growing company * Options for career advancement * Learning and development opportunities * Flexible working environment * Competitive salaries based on experience **Equal Opportunity Employer:** Amach is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions on the basis of merit. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. This job description is intended to convey essential responsibilities and qualifications for this role, but it is not an exhaustive list of tasks that an employee may be required to perform. If you are passionate about driving customer success, advising on strategic solutions, and contributing to product innovation, we would love to hear from you! **Not for you?** Check out all of our open positions in our **careers page** and follow us on **LinkedIn** for future opportunities. P.S. Share this with friends and co\-workers! Don't be afraid they'll steal it from you, if you're amazing and smart we'll find a role for you. We are growing fast and we are always looking for talented people. At Amach, we strive to be an inclusive community of open\-minded individuals with different backgrounds and we are committed to fostering, cultivating and preserving a culture of diversity, equity and inclusion. We strongly believe that a diversity of experience and background is essential to create a fulfilling environment and better solutions for our people and our customers. All Amach employees and contractors are expected to honour this policy and act to ensure that every individual is respected in the workplace. **Your personal data** Amach will process your personal information in accordance with the EU's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). We will comply with data protection law and principles, which means that your data will be: * Used lawfully, fairly and in a transparent way * Collected only for valid purposes and not used in any way that is incompatible with those purposes * Relevant to the purposes we have told you about and limited only to those purposes * Accurate and kept up to date * Kept only as long as necessary for the purposes we have told you about * Kept securely If you would like to contact us about your data, please use the following address: info@amach.com

title: Delivery Technician I
description: Apply **Description** We offer competitive pay based on experience, recognizing the value and skills each team member brings to our organization. **Job Summary/Job Purpose** The Delivery Technician I effectively interacts with patients/clients, their families, caregivers, and members of the healthcare/HME team in providing professional home medical equipment products and services. Provides delivery set\-up, initial instruction, and pick\-up of HME. **Job Duties / Essential Functions** * Deliver, set up and pick up durable medical equipment, oxygen products, and supplies to home care patients and commercial accounts following all company policies and procedures. * Provide patient/client with clear instruction on the proper use of equipment. * Able to assess home safety, accessibility and patient/customer status and needs, communicate with supervisor or Quality team when patient/environment has changed. * Partner with dispatch department to create most efficient delivery routes. * Practice safe driving habits and ensure proper oversight of vehicle and equipment while on a delivery or pick\-up. * Follow proper safety precautions, both on the road and within patient/client locations. Perform proper infection control procedures. * Treat patients with kindness and compassion. * On a rotating basis, ability to be on\-call and respond to inquiries or concerns within company guidelines. * All other duties assigned. **Requirements** **Skills / Qualifications:** * High School or GED * Valid driver’s license with no significant infractions per the DMV within the last 3 years * Previous delivery experience preferred * Ability to obtain/maintain DOT medical card * Strong communication and customer service skills * Organized and detail oriented * Ability to use Tablet/iPad to navigate through internal system/application * Basic math skills **Physical Requirements / Working Conditions** These are physical and mental requirements of the position as it is typically performed. Inability to meet one or more of these physical or mental requirements will not automatically disqualify a candidate or employee from the position. Upon request for a reasonable accommodation, the Company may be able to adjust or excuse one or more of these requirements, depending on the requirement, the essential functions to which it relates, and the proposed accommodation. * Ability to pass DOT medical exam * Lifting up to 60 pounds * Stand, sit, walk for long periods of time * Ability to use dolly or hand cart to push/pull larger items of significant weight * Kneel, stoop, bend, or crawl * Enter and exit vehicle safely, using step or handle

title: Design Engineer
description: **DAS Engineer (In\-Building Cellular Coverage)** **Company Overview** Sigma Wireless Communications specialises in providing mission\-critical communication solutions. Our clients include public safety agencies, enterprises, healthcare institutions, utilities, and transportation providers in Ireland. Additionally, we serve clients directly and indirectly in over 20 countries worldwide **Role Overview** As a DAS Engineer specializing in in\-building cellular systems (4G/5G), you will be responsible for proposing, designing, and delivering wireless communication solutions that meet customer requirements. Here are the key responsibilities **Understanding Customer Requirements** * Provide expert advice to clients and the sales team * Collaborate with clients to determine their communication needs * Analyse project specifications and constraints * Provide expert advice to clients and the sales team * Generate proposals and assist in contract negotiations **Detailed Site Surveys** * Perform comprehensive site surveys to gather data on signal levels \& RF propagation * Gather site\-specific information for system installation **Radio Coverage Design and Optimization** * Design radio coverage solutions for cellular networks * Optimise coverage, reliability, and capacity * Propose tailored solutions for challenging environments such as urban areas, tunnels, and critical infrastructure **System Proposals and Design Documentation** * Prepare system proposals and pricing documents * Prepare comprehensive system design/completion documentation * Include technical, implementation and operational details **System Build and Test** * Coordinate the installation of in\-building cellular systems * Undertake the commissioning and testing of systems. **Qualifications and Skills** * Education: Bachelor’s or University Degree in Technical, Engineering, Computer Science, or related fields. * Technical Proficiency: Knowledge of cellular technologies (4G/5G) and RF bands, etc, for in\-building cellular systems. * Familiarity with relevant equipment suppliers including DAS, repeaters and small\-cell solutions. * Proficiency with standard in\-building cellular tools such as iBwave/Ranplan, TEMS/NEMO, etc. * Familiarity with RF protocols, security, and traffic/capacity modelling, and ICNIRP compliance for RF health and safety. * Understanding of wireless communications fundamentals (both public and private networks). **Desired Experience** * 3 years’ experience in technical sales, design or delivery of enterprise network solutions. * Experience presenting proposals to customers for both technical and executive audience. * Proven ability to work with Account Managers or partners in closing sales opportunities. **Why Join Sigma?** Impact: Shape future communication solutions that directly impact public safety and enterprise customers. * Collaboration: Work closely with clients, sales teams, and industry experts. * Innovation: Stay at the forefront of wireless technology trends. * Competitive salary and performance\-based bonuses. * Comprehensive benefits package. * Professional development opportunities. * Opportunity to shape our strategy and the future of wireless communication

title: DevOps Engineer
description: We are partnering with a leading insurance client on a large\-scale transformation programme and are seeking a DevOps / QA Engineer to support the delivery of resilient CI/CD infrastructure, automated testing, and deployment reliability across critical enterprise platforms. This role sits between DevOps, platform engineering, and quality assurance teams, with a strong focus on automation, platform resilience, and continuous improvement. While not a formal management role, this position will suit someone who enjoys taking ownership, driving standards, and acting as a technical lead across CI/CD and QA initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Build, optimise, and maintain robust CI/CD infrastructure and deployment pipelines * Develop and maintain automated smoke, integration, performance, and security testing within the deployment lifecycle * Design and implement automated rollback and failover procedures to minimise downtime and improve platform resilience * Partner closely with engineering teams to identify build failures and drive technical remediation activities * Support continuous improvement across deployment automation, testing standards, and release quality * Prepare and deliver weekly system demonstrations covering infrastructure health, performance benchmarks, and security scanning metrics * Contribute to best practices across DevOps, QA automation, and release engineering processes **Required Experience** * 4\+ years’ experience across DevOps, CI/CD engineering, and QA/testing environments * Strong experience with CI/CD pipelines, deployment automation, and version control workflows * Hands\-on experience with automated testing frameworks for integration and infrastructure testing * Strong scripting capability with experience writing reusable automation and verification scripts * Experience troubleshooting deployment failures and supporting remediation activities across engineering teams * Strong communication skills with the ability to work cross\-functionally across technical teams **Desirable Experience** * Experience within enterprise or regulated environments * Exposure to cloud infrastructure and Infrastructure as Code practices * Experience supporting highly available production systems **How to Apply** If you are a DevOps / QA Engineer looking for a role with strong technical ownership please apply with your latest CV.

title: DevOps Engineer | €65k
description: Most DevOps roles are about operating systems that already exist. This one is about building the infrastructure that doesn’t exist yet. It's powering AI\-driven drug discovery through large\-scale simulation and computational science. We’re looking for a **hands\-on Platform Engineer who builds systems from the ground up** , not someone who configures tools built by others. **What you’ll do** * Build containerised systems using **Docker / Podman** * Design multi\-service environments using **Docker Compose / Swarm** * Build CI/CD pipelines end\-to\-end ( **GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins** ) * Develop automation and internal tooling used by engineering and R\&D teams * Work at Linux level (systemd, networking, shell scripting, debugging) * Support compute\-heavy scientific and AI workloads **Mandatory requirements** * Proven experience **building with Docker (not just running containers)** * Experience **designing and building CI/CD pipelines from scratch** * Strong **Linux fundamentals (shell scripting, networking, systemd)** * Hands\-on ability to build and structure infrastructure systems independently **Nice to have** * Python for automation and tooling * Observability tools (Prometheus, Grafana, alerting systems) * Exposure to HPC, AI, simulation, or research computing environments * Infrastructure\-as\-Code (Ansible or similar) **You are not a fit if you:** * Only use managed DevOps/cloud platforms * Mainly configure existing pipelines and infrastructure * Don’t have strong Linux\-level troubleshooting ability * Prefer ticket\-based or maintenance\-style work **Why this role** This is not a DevOps maintenance role. It’s a **builder role inside a deep\-tech environment where infrastructure directly enables scientific discovery and AI\-driven drug development** . If you like designing systems properly from scratch, this will feel like home. APPLY NOW!

title: Digital & Social Media Executive (B2C Retail Content)
description: **Location:** Citywest, Dublin (Hybrid options available) **Package:** €40k – €45,000 \+ 10% Bonus \+ Pension \+ Life Assurance **Requirements:** B2C Content, ideally in Retail, \& Full Drivers Licence needed **The Opportunity: Drive the Digital Voice of a National Irish Brand** Are you a creative marketer who loves to get out from behind the desk? We are representing a well\-established **Irish retail success story** with a nationwide network of over 100\+ stores. They are looking for a **Digital \& Social Media Executive** to be the creative digital engine for their brand. This is a content\-first role where you will travel to locations across the country, capturing the authentic stories, expertise, and community connections that make this group a household name in the **retail sector.** **The Role** Reporting to the Marketing Manager, you will lead the planning, execution, and optimisation of digital activity across a major national brand. * **Content Creation:** Produce engaging photography, graphics, and short\-form video (Reels/TikToks) that showcase products and store activity. * **Social Media Management:** Own the end\-to\-end content calendar, scheduling, and community engagement across all brand channels. * **On\-the\-Road Storytelling:** Travel nationwide to capture “real\-life” content—from staff expertise to local community projects and initiatives. * **Campaign Impact:** Support national retail promotions and seasonal trading through email marketing and paid social activity. * **Collaboration:** Work with local store owners and external partners to ensure all digital activity aligns with the brand’s identity and mission. **Requirements** * **Road Ready:** A **Full Irish Driving Licence** is essential as the role involves travel to stores nationwide. * **Experience:** 2–3 years in a Digital Marketing or Social Media role. * **Sector Savvy:** Experience in a **Retail or Trade environment** is a distinct advantage. * **Content Skills:** A natural eye for engaging visuals and strong copywriting skills. Experience with video editing tools is a plus. * **Education:** Third\-level qualification in Marketing or a related discipline. **Why Join?** * **Competitive Package:** €40k–€45,000, plus a performance bonus of up to 10%. * **Future\-Proofing:** 8% Company Pension contribution and Life Assurance. * **Work\-Life Balance:** Incremental annual leave plus two additional company days. * **Flexibility:** Initially site\-based with h **ybrid working options** available following successful probation.

title: Digital Marketing Executive
description: Spanish Point is one of Ireland’s leading indigenous tech companies with three core focus areas: * The global music industry, through our market\-leading Matching Engine product. * European SaaS providers through our innovative SmartAIM Library product and our associated SmartAIM Framework services. * Digital transformation services for medium to large commercial and public sector organisations in Ireland, through our partnership with Microsoft. We are going through a period of sustained growth and are looking to recruit an ambitious Digital Marketing Platforms Executive to help continue that journey. The Digital Marketing Platforms Executive will be responsible for implementation, optimisation, and on\-going delivery of world\-class digital platforms that support marketing operations for large\-scale, multi\-channel campaigns. The successful candidate will be responsible for co\-ordination with in\-house web development, Azure, and Dynamics 365 teams, web digital design and UX agencies, website content agencies, and marketing department team members. **RESPONSIBILITIES:** * Plan, implement, and optimise digital marketing platforms across website, CRM, email, SEO, and digital content platforms. * Support the delivery of digital projects by working cross\-functionally with colleagues in Spanish Point, to identify and capture development requirements for our digital platforms and translate them into project briefs and tasks. * Ensure support and delivery of our strategy by managing the development roadmap across all digital platforms with external development and design agencies. * Deliver digital platform strategies with formats and functionality optimised to engage and convert target audiences outlined in our audience segmentation strategy. * Ensure our digital platforms are live and accessible by monitoring domain registrations, SSL certs, and DNS records. * Provide training and support to both internal and external users on Spanish Point’s digital platforms as needed. * Be the in\-house owner of the Sitecore CMS, supported by an in\-house development team and an external Sitecore partnership team. * Ensure colleagues are informed of updates by communicating plans and releases on Spanish Point’s digital platforms to internal users. * Maintain a close working relationship with the IT team and keep them informed of any relevant projects and developments from the Marketing Team to anticipate and plan infrastructure requirements. * Working with the marketing team, champion a customer focused mindset, with win:win content strategy implementation based on insight and analytics. Monitor performance, analyse results, and make recommendations to optimise digital platforms and marketing content. * Track and improve platform performance against set KPIs, including utilisation, engagement rates, conversion rates, and lead generation. * Work closely with Head of Marketing to run reporting for Marketing Qualified Leads (MQLs). Support development and implementation campaign approaches to drive lead generation. * Monitor budget allocation across digital platforms to achieve maximum impact of marketing spend. * Keep the organisation future proofed by researching new innovations and best practice. Stay up to date with B2B digital marketing and technology industry trends. Recommend new platforms and marketing campaign ideas to maintain a competitive edge. * Monitor data accuracy and lead qualification in the CRM, as well as integration of marketing platforms including Spanish Point Technologies website properties. Implement changes as required to support marketing campaign reporting needs. **SKILLS, KNOWLEDGE AND EXPERIENCE:** * You will have at least 3 years of experience in technology related product and services marketing (UX, marketing technology project management, digital and content marketing campaigns). * Proven track record of flawless project management and delivery for enterprise\-grade marketing projects and systems. * Proven experience with SEO, landing page and conversion rate optimisation. * Hands\-on experience with marketing platforms including email marketing, CRM systems, website CMS and SEO management platforms. * Proficiency in Google Analytics, Microsoft Dynamics 365, Semrush and other tracking tools. * You will have excellent English language presentation, written, and verbal communication skills, with an eye for quality and attention to detail. * Previous experience working within a Microsoft partner ecosystem, SaaS, or B2B Marketing. **NICE TO HAVE:** * Familiarity with website Sitecore XM Cloud / Sitecore AI CMS. * Familiarity with email marketing including ClickDimensions or similar B2B automation and ecomms platforms. * Demonstrable experience collaborating with internal cross\-functional teams and external partners. * Previous working experience as a product owner and/or knowledge of ‘Agile’ process and principles advantageous. * Bachelor's degree in marketing and/or Technology. **BENEFITS:** * Working arrangement: Hybrid – minimum of 3 days per week in office. * Perks include: Financial support for professional development, Annual Performance Reviews, 24/7 Employee Assistance, Health Insurance, Life Assurance, Cycle to Work Scheme, Sports \& Social Activities (Join our Green Team, Yoga or Hiking Club), Free Canteen, Referral Bonus Scheme **Application Process** We are accepting applications until August with a plan to complete the recruitment process by the end of that month. Please send applications and/or questions to recruitment@spanishpoint.ie with a subject containing “Digital Marketing Platforms Executive”.

title: ESB Subsidiary Company Secretary
description: **Position Description** ESB is a purpose\-led organisation focused on creating a brighter future and leading the transition to reliable, affordable, low\-carbon energy. To support delivery of ESB’s Net Zero by 2040 Strategy, ESB has a growing number of joint ventures, subsidiary \& associate companies (\>200\) operating in electricity generation, supply and related activities, international consulting, IT/Telecoms and asset management. These companies are primarily Irish and UK based, with a small number based in Europe and the far East. This role offers an opportunity to join ESB’s Company Secretariat team and play an important part in supporting high standards of corporate governance across the organisation. The successful applicant will be part of a small team and will have responsibility for managing a defined portfolio of subsidiary companies. The role requires strong organisation, sound judgement, attention to detail and the ability to build trusted relationships with a wide range of stakeholders. Candidates for this position are expected to have a third level qualification in a related field, wide experience of best practice in the application of corporate governance principles, and a superior record of successfully managing relationships across a spectrum of corporate activities. **Key Responsibilities** * Ensure timely compliance with all statutory and regulatory requirements, including corporate filings, beneficial ownership obligations and maintenance of statutory records. * Coordinate Board, Committee and Shareholder meetings, including scheduling, issuing notices, preparing agendas, compiling board packs and drafting minutes and resolutions. * Maintain high standards of corporate governance, ensuring adherence to governance frameworks, company constitutions and related policies and procedures. * Act as a trusted point of contact on company law and governance matters, working closely with the in\-house legal team and responding to queries from internal and external stakeholders. * Build and maintain strong relationships with Directors and support director induction, training and ongoing awareness of their duties and responsibilities. * Support the production of year\-end financial statements for subsidiary companies in collaboration with the Financial Operations Team. * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives within the Company Secretariat, including digitisation, new ways of working and support for internal projects. **Essential** **Experience and Qualifications** * Third level qualification in a related field (i.e. company law, corporate governance, legal qualification). * Minimum of 5 years' relevant work experience in a company secretarial or related role in an Irish or UK jurisdiction. * Strong knowledge of public sector governance standards and procedures. * Proven ability to communicate effectively with stakeholders at all levels and translate complex material into meaningful information. * High standard of secretarial skills, technical and analytical abilities. * Ability to work proactively and independently, applying discretion when dealing with confidential information. * Proven track record in working in team environment with a focus on delivering results and working to tight deadlines. **Desirable** * A corporate secretarial qualification from the Chartered Governance Institute is highly desirable. * The role involves some travel, so a driving license is highly desirable. **Location** This position will be based in F27, ESB’s Head Office, Dublin 2\. *Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration **at least two days per week** . All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business.* **Reporting To** This role will report to the Manager Company Secretariat. **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €59,900 \- €70,500 per annum (depending on experience) **Closing Date** *6th July 2026* **Interview** Candidates who are shortlisted for interview will complete a Behavioural Interview based on each of the following Behaviours, which underpin how we work at ESB and are critical to delivering our strategy: * Accountable and Ethical * Collaborative * Growing and Adaptive * Proactive * Respectful and Supportive More information available on Our People Promise **Notes** Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role. The successful candidate will be required to evidence their right to work in the Republic of Irelandprior to commencement of employment.

title: Ecommerce Executive
description: As an Ecommerce Executive at Home Store \+ More, you'll be responsible for an entire online category and its products, helping us to increase transactions on the Home Store \+ More websites. It'll be your job to improve onsite experiences that help customers find what they're looking for as quickly as possible. **What you will do:** * Creation and merchandising of new product for the website. * You'll complete daily web checks and test sales to ensure the website functions as intended for the customer. * Work extensively on onsite search to ensure the best results for the customers. * Responsible for the end\-to\-end online journey for promotions within your category. * Continual improvement and tracking of your category’s keyword position in Google. * Use our websites Content Management System to optimise on\-site search results, site filters, navigation and promotions. Plus designing and creating new web pages \& articles. * You'll communicate regularly with our Buying Department and Marketing Department. * Help test new web features before they are released to our live site. **What we need in this role:** * Hardworking enthusiastic person with an attention to detail. * Willingness to work as part of a team, supporting one another to achieve targets. * Desire to learn new skills and adapt to an exciting, constantly changing world of ECommerce. * An understanding or enthusiasm to learn our website’s Content Management System. * A confident communicator. * An ability to manage and prioritise diverse tasks and time well, working calmly under pressure. * Ability to work well alone or as part of a team. Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €27,500\.00\-€34,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * On\-site parking * Sick pay * Store discount * Work from home Work Location: Hybrid remote in DUBLIN 12, County Dublin

title: Electrical Engineer
description: **The role** As Electrical Engineer, you will support the technical delivery of electrical building services packages from design review through to construction, commissioning and handover. You will work closely with senior engineers, project managers, design teams, subcontractors and suppliers to ensure that electrical works are coordinated, compliant, buildable and delivered to Designer Group's high standards. This role will suit someone who is technically curious, organised, eager to learn and comfortable working as part of a busy project delivery team. **You will:** * Review drawings, specifications and schedules to support compliance with project requirements, standards and industry guidance. * Support the review of coordinated construction drawings and technical documentation. * Assist with RFIs, technical queries and design clarifications. * Review specialist supplier packages against project specifications and relevant standards. * Prepare, track and manage technical submittals for client approval. * Support procurement schedules in conjunction with the procurement team. * Use engineering software such as Dialux, Relux, Amtech ProDesign, Navisworks, AutoCAD or similar tools where required. * Assist with BCAR inspection requirements and coordinate with the quality team where needed. * Support LEED and BREEAM requirements and related technical submittals where applicable. * Assist with operation and maintenance manuals and handover documentation. * Attend client demonstrations, inspections and commissioning activities. * Provide technical support to the wider project team during construction, commissioning and handover. **Requirements** **We are looking for someone with:** * A Level 7 degree or higher in Electrical Engineering, Electrical Services Engineering, Building Services Engineering or a related discipline. * 2\-4 years' experience in electrical engineering, electrical project engineering, building services or a similar role. * A good understanding of electrical systems from a design, construction or installation perspective. * Knowledge of electrical services such as power distribution, lighting, containment, fire alarm systems, emergency lighting and general building services. * Ability to support project teams with technical queries, design coordination, site issues, submittals, inspections and handover documentation. * Strong communication, organisational and problem\-solving skills. * A team\-focused approach and the ability to work with senior engineers, project managers, design teams, site teams, subcontractors and suppliers. **Desirable Experience:** Experience with any of the following would be an advantage, but is not essential: * Dialux, Relux, Amtech, Navisworks, AutoCAD or similar engineering software. * Irish electrical standards, fire alarm standards and emergency lighting standards. * BCAR, LEED or BREEAM requirements. * M\&E contracting or building services project delivery. **Benefits** At Designer Group, our people are central to our continued growth. We invest in career development, technical training and professional progression. We are an Engineers Ireland accredited employer for CPD and actively support our engineers on their pathway to chartership. Our Head of Engineering began his career with Designer Group as a Graduate Electrical Engineer. That progression reflects the type of career path we want to create for ambitious engineers across the business. You will be joining a company with a strong project pipeline, international growth, and a culture built around our core values: Safe. Trust. Respect. Challenge. Deliver. **What we offer** * Competitive salary * Healthcare * Pension * Career progression * Engineers Ireland accredited CPD support * Pathway to chartership * Exposure to major M\&E projects * International career opportunities * Supportive team environment **Apply now** If you are an Electrical Engineer looking to take the next step in your career with a growing international M\&E contractor, we would like to hear from you. Apply today and take the next step in building your future with Designer Group. Designer Group is an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from suitably qualified candidates regardless of sex, race, disability, age, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, religion or belief, marital status, pregnancy or maternity.

title: Financial Advisor
description: **Experienced Financial Adviser \- Mullingar** Full Time **Permanent** position Hybrid Role \- Mullingar Area **What we offer** We have embraced a hybrid working model for most of our positions, which means that you can enjoy a balanced approach of working from home for part of the week and working from the office for the remainder of the week. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including competitive salaries and bonuses, robust Learning and Development support, excellent Defined Contribution pension and comprehensive Wellbeing initiatives and support to name but a few. Further details on our benefits package can be accessed here Benefits (life\-careers.com) **Company Values** At Irish Life, our purpose is to help people build better futures. Our company values shape everything we do and underpin our vision of being the driving force behind Ireland’s financial, physical, and mental well\-being. We believe in **doing the right thing** —acting with integrity and making fair, responsible decisions. We **aim high** , setting ambitious goals and **find better ways** to innovate and improve. Our **customers are at the heart of everything we do** and we are dedicated to understanding their needs and exceeding their expectations. Achieving this is only possible when **we work best together** —in a supportive, collaborative environment where every idea and contribution is valued. **Role Overview** Our Financial Planning distribution which encompasses our Financial Advice Team, our Bancassurance business, our Worksite proposition and our Digital Advice team is currently embarking on an ambitious growth agenda to match changing customer attitudes and expectations around the provision of financial advice. To support this growth, we are looking for financial advisers to provide professional financial advice to individual customers and businesses on their financial needs. We are specifically seeking candidate who is based in the Mullingar area. The role operates either a hybrid working model through a blend of face to face and remote advice tailored to the customer’s needs. We also have in\-branch adviser roles in Bancassurance nationwide. The provision of financial advice is supported through the Irish Life financial advice process, with adherence to Irish Life compliance standards. **Key Accountabilities** * Develop client relationships with new and existing customers across multiple sources of business. * Provide professional best advice in developing clear financial plans for new and existing clients while delivering superior customer satisfaction. * Establish and develop relationships with small and medium sized companies to offer financial advice within the company. * Conduct professional financial reviews with customers in person and over Microsoft Teams and recommend products in adherence to Irish Life compliance standards. * Work to agreed targets and standards. * Ensure compliance with Company policies, regulatory, professional and legal requirements * Proactively protect, maintain and develop existing Client Bank * Communicate complex financial matters to customers in layman terms. * Adhere to appropriate risk management procedures to control, monitor and report on business activities. * Keep up to date with all changes in the commercial environment and completes Continual Professional Development (CPD) on an on\-going basis. **Minimum Requirements:** * Fully qualified QFA as a minimum. * Relevant 3rd Level Qualification would be an advantage. * Holding or on the path to the Retirement Planning Advisor designation would be an advantage. * As this role requires travel between locations applicants must hold a full, valid driver’s licence. **Desired Knowledge/Experience/Skills** *The successful candidate should ideally possess the following skills and knowledge:* * Significant experience providing financial advice to individuals and businesses with a proven track record of sales. * Experience in a customer facing role and be commercially aware. * Detailed knowledge and understanding of regulatory and compliance requirements of an Irish insurance company and the Central Bank. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with new and existing customers. * Possess QFA (have passed all ‘Qualified Financial Advisor’ exams) and be up to date on all CPD requirements. * Ability to work on own initiative and as part of a team. * Competent in providing financial advice on retail products * Ability to present a strong professional image of Irish Life **Fitness \& Probity** This role is a ‘controlled function’ as defined by the Central Bank Reform Act 2010 Regulations 2011\. Any appointment will be conditional on the company being satisfied that the appointee meets the requirements as set out in the Fitness and Probity standards issued by the Central Bank. This requires the company to complete prescribed due diligence to assess the appointee’s fitness and probity. Further details on this due diligence process are available from ILFS Human Resources. **About us** Irish Life is one of Ireland’s largest financial institutions with a presence across a range of financial services markets. We are one of Ireland’s leading financial services groups with over 1\.4 million customers. For over 80 years, we’ve been helping people to look after their life insurance, pension and investment needs, and more recently their health insurance. It is a subsidiary of Great\-West Lifeco and a member of the Power Financial Corporation group of companies. We are proud of our culture of developing and supporting the growth and professional development of our people. The focus has resulted in a team of top talent with high standards, positioning us at the forefront of our industry. The company reserves the right to draw up a shortlist as part of the selection process. Where Agency assistance is required the Irish Life Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to Irish Life by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this role. Irish Life is proud to be an Equal Opportunities employer. We have created an environment of inclusion and growth to help people achieve their potential, to ensure they can bring their whole selves to work and to feel valued. We celebrate diversity of thought and perspective and want all of our employees to feel valued, respected and supported. We have built a culture that promotes equality and inclusion for all of our current and future employees and strive to ensure that our behaviours, processes and practices are aligned to this culture. If you require any accommodations during the recruitment process please contact lifecareers@irishlife.ie and we will be delighted to ensure you are fully supported to be your best. **Irish Life Financial Services** supports Equal Opportunity. Irish Life Assurance plc is regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland.

title: Foreman - Structures
description: As a **Foreman \- Structures** **,** you’ll be at the heart of an exciting new West Apron Vehicle Underpass project at **Dublin Airport** . Join our dynamic team and make a difference! The **Foreman \- Structures** will be responsible for the day‑to‑day supervision and coordination of structural construction activities on a live project at Dublin Airport. Reporting to the Site Agent – Structures, the role ensures that all structural works are carried out safely, to the required quality standards, and in accordance with programme and airport operational constraints, while leading site crews and subcontractors. **About Us:** At Sacyr UK and Ireland, we’re passionate about creating a better world through our projects. As a **Foreman \- Structures** , you’ll play a crucial role in our team. Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and designed for everyone to thrive. We don’t just say it – we live it, we’re proud to have been named a **Sunday Times Top 100 Employer in both 2024 and 2025** , and to be accredited as a **Living Wage** and **Disability Confiden** t employer. **About you:** If your values align with ours, and you are passionate about being part of our team, read on! Our team is professional, great communicators, adaptable, full of integrity and generally a fabulous bunch. If this sounds like you, and you can demonstrate the required skills and experience, we want to hear from you. **Our benefits:** * 34 days’ annual leave * Employee Assistance Programme * Life assurance – 4x annual salary * Pension scheme (details above) * Cycle to Work scheme * Eye care vouchers – eye tests and glasses where required, including safety eyewear * Annual health checks with Randox Health, including half\-yearly check\-ins * Enhanced annual leave * Company\-funded language lessons – Spanish/English * Company\-funded training: training, certifications, and memberships in accredited bodies related to the position * Lighthouse Club membership * Discount shopping vouchers * Opportunity for additional professional development * And more benefits to be implemented **Key Responsibilities include:** **Supervision \& Site Management** * Supervise site crews and subcontractors carrying out structural works, including reinforced concrete, formwork, steel fixing, concrete pours, and associated activities * Plan and organise daily works in line with the short‑term programme and site priorities * Allocate labour, plant, and materials efficiently to meet programme targets * Ensure works are coordinated with adjacent trades and disciplines **Health, Safety \& Environment** * Enforce compliance with all HSEQ requirements, site rules, and airport safety procedures * Ensure method statements and risk assessments are fully understood and followed on site * Conduct daily briefings, toolbox talks, and safety inspections * Maintain high standards of housekeeping in structural work areas * Ensure works comply with airside safety rules, permits, and access requirements where applicable **Quality Control** * Ensure structural works are completed in accordance with drawings, specifications, ITPs, and quality standards * Carry out first‑line quality checks prior to engineer and client inspections * Support concrete inspections, pre‑pour checks, and snag management * Ensure defects and non‑conformances are addressed promptly **Programme \& Productivity** * Drive productivity and monitor progress against the programme * Highlight delays, constraints, and resource issues to the Site Agent – Structures * Support recovery planning and resequencing of works when required * Maintain accurate daily records of works completed and resources used **Coordination \& Communication** * Work closely with the Site Agent – Structures and Construction Engineers to deliver structural works * Liaise with subcontractors, suppliers, plant operators, and logistics teams * Communicate upcoming works, constraints, and site requirements to crews * Participate in coordination meetings and planning sessions as required **Documentation \& Reporting** * Maintain daily site diaries, progress reports, and allocation records * Assist with permits, inspections, and sign‑off documentation * Support handover activities and snag close‑out **Essential criteria:** * Legal right to work in the Republic of Ireland * Willingness to complete a 5\-year background screening as part of the hiring process * Trade background with significant experience as a Structures Foreman or Chargehand * Proven experience working on reinforced concrete and structural works * Experience supervising direct labour and subcontractors * Valid Safe Pass and ability to obtain airport security clearance * CSCS Supervisor / Safety Supervisor or equivalent * Strong knowledge of structural construction methods * Proven ability to supervise crews in safety‑critical environments * Good understanding of drawings, method statements, and construction sequencing * Strong leadership and communication skills * High level of safety awareness and attention to detail **Desirable criteria:** * Experience on airport, aviation, or other high‑security live environment * Experience on large‑scale infrastructure or civil engineering projects In return, you will be working on a high profile 'once in a lifetime' project where you will be supported by a collaborative team in a learning environment. We offer local and global career opportunities and whilst this project is based in Dublin our teams are truly global! We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We encourage applications from all backgrounds and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We are open to making reasonable adjustments to enable all applicants to participate in the recruitment process. Please let us know if you need any adjustments as part of the application process. Ready to be part of our transformative journey? Apply now and contribute to building a better future! **\#SacyrOnCourse**

title: General Maintenance Operative
description: General Maintenance Operative: Oberstown, Lusk **(Own transport essential due to lack of public transport)** Aramark is currently recruiting a reliable and hands\-on General Maintenance Operative to join our team.
