title: Graduate Sales Advisor
description: **Location: Dublin with local travel** **Full\-time role** **Salary: €30,000 \+ uncapped commission** **The Opportunity** Launch a high\-impact sales career with immediate responsibility, accelerated earnings, and fast\-track progression within a dynamic, fast\-growing Irish\-owned company. * Immediate commercial exposure * Structured Training Programme * Dual experience: Field \+ Telesales * High earning potential (uncapped commission) * Fast\-track progression * Strong, fast\-growing Irish\-owned organisation **Overview** We are seeking ambitious and driven graduates to join the team as Graduate Sales Advisors. The role offers a unique blend of telesales (9 months) and field sales experience (3 months), providing exposure to multiple sales channels and a strong foundation for a long\-term career in sales. **The Role** An entry\-level commercial role for graduates motivated by targets, progression, and financial rewards. The position provides real accountability from day one, with clear pathways into advanced sales and leadership roles. **Key Responsibilities** *Telesales (Inside Sales):* * Engage with customers via phone * Generate leads through outbound calling and follow up on inbound enquiries * Build relationships, handle objections, and close deals * Present products and services and manage a sales pipeline *Field Sales (Outside Sales):* * Conduct door\-to\-door sales visits to residential customers and multiple property owners with a focus on high conversion rate. * Execute strategic sales approaches to maximise customer engagement. * Build strong relationships and Promote and sell PrepayPower products/services. * Identify new business opportunities ***General Responsibilities:*** * Achieve sales targets and KPIs * Maintain CRM records * Collaborate with teams **Skills \& Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree (all disciplines considered) * Strong communication skills * Motivated and target\-driven * Full clean driving licence is preferred * Results\-oriented and proactive * Confident communicator * Resilient mindset * Willingness to learn * Customer\-focused ### **Benefits** * **Competitive salary and bonus structure** * **Private Pension Contribution Scheme** with Zurich Life * **Discounted Health Insurance \- Group Scheme** with Laya Healthcare * **Employee Assistance Program** provided by TELUS Health * **Free Online GP Services** via Webdoctor.ie * **Excellent career progression opportunities** in a growing company with strong market presence * **Fast\-paced, friendly team environment** * **Continuous training and development** * **Annual leave that increases with service** * **Exclusive PrepayPower Staff Tariff** * **Modern offices** with excellent facilities in Sandyford * **Active CSR, Wellness, and Social initiatives** plus great social events * **Refer\-a\-Friend Scheme** * **TaxSaver Travel Tickets \& Bike\-to\-Work Scheme** ### **About PrepayPower** PrepayPower is the leading provider of Pay\-As\-You\-Go electricity and gas in Ireland. Since 2011, we have been disrupting the energy market by empowering customers with total control and transparency over their electricity costs through our app. PrepayPower's mission is to give people total control and complete transparency over their energy costs. Rather than the traditional market model of short\-term discounts with significant contract terms, we help customers achieve long\-term savings on their electricity, gas, oil and broadband by putting them in control. Customer satisfaction is at the heart of everything we do. Our commitment to providing exceptional service has earned us the title of Ireland's highest\-rated energy supplier on Trustpilot. In 2025, together with Yuno Energy, we proudly reached over 350,000 customer connections. **Why work at PrepayPower** Ranked as a top employer for seven consecutive years by Great Places to Work Ireland, we offer ambitious individuals a fast\-paced and challenging work environment with enormous opportunities for growth. PrepayPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to treating all our employees, customers, and stakeholders with dignity and respect. Therefore, we value diverse talent and strive to create an inclusive workplace that delivers exceptional, innovative services for our customers. We encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences. PrepayPower is part of the Yuno Group alongside a suite of brands, including Yuno Energy and firmus Energy. With almost 600 employees and growing, we are the market leader for Pay\-as\-You\-Go Electricity, Gas, and Broadband in Ireland. Join us and become part of a dynamic, ambitious team that's putting control in the hands of our customers. **Privacy** By applying for this role, you agree to PrepayPower processing your personal data for recruitment purposes. We handle all applications in line with our Privacy Notice. You can view our privacy notice here Careers \- Privacy Policy \| PrePayPower for more information.

title: Graduate Sales Executive (Irish Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Graduate Trainee Underwriter
description: **Job Title:** Graduate Trainee Underwriter – Surety **Reporting to:** Head of Surety \- Ireland **Location:** Kill, Co. Kildare. Ireland **Position Type:** Full time. Permanent. Initially during the training process, it is 5 days a week in the office but after successful completion of training it is 3 days in the office and 2 working from home. Overview: **Why** **Tokio Marine HCC?** Standing still is not an option in the current world of Insurance. TMHCC are one of the world’s leading Specialty Insurers. With deep expertise in our chosen lines of business, our unparalleled track record and a solid balance sheet, TMHCC evaluates and manages risk like no one else in the industry. Looking beyond profit, empowering our people and delivering on our commitments are at the core of our customer values, and so is a desire to grow and provide creative and innovative solutions to our clients. Are you a motivated and ambitious graduate in Law, Finance, or Business looking to apply your skills in a commercial, global environment—without following the traditional practice route? Do you have the ability to analyse financial information and understand how financial statements tell the story of a business? If so, this is a fantastic opportunity to build a rewarding career with strong earning potential, outside of a traditional legal or financial practice, with full study support provided. **Job Purpose:** We are looking for a motivated and analytical graduate to join our European Surety Underwriting Team within Tokio Marine HCC (TMHCC). This role offers a unique opportunity to develop technical and commercial expertise in a niche area of insurance, working alongside experienced underwriters across a wide European network. The Surety division at TMHCC provides bonds and guarantees relating to contractual and regulatory obligations for clients based globally, with a primary focus on Western Europe. You will work as part of a collaborative, international team with offices across: London, Leicester, Paris, Amsterdam, Copenhagen, Madrid, Milan \& Frankfurt. As a result, some travel will be required as part of this role. This role offers a clear and structured career pathway, with the opportunity to progress into an Underwriting position within approximately 3–5 years, depending on performance. This will be done by giving you exposure to complex, international cases and clients. There is a strong long\-term earnings potential as you advance within the business **Key Responsibilities:** * Underwriting of surety risks in line with agreed strategy and personal authority limits. * Review and agreement of legal documents (such as bond forms, security documents, contracts and other agreements). * Building relationships with internal and external stakeholders and clients. Liaising effectively with clients and brokers. * Gather relevant and sufficient information both financial and other, to assess financial strength of existing and potential clients. * Researching the internet and other media to find relevant and up to date information. * Analyse information gained from various sources to add to reports. * Assist in internal and external meetings as required. * Be able to provide explanation and reasons for decisions made. * Create and present reports as required. * Maintain company records and databases. * Identify and escalate any potential risks. * Assist with market and product development. * Other reasonable duties as instructed by the Senior Underwriter/Head of Department. **Skills and Experience Specification:** **Essential:** * Honours Bachelor’s degree, 2:1 or above, preferably in Law, Finance, Business or a related analytical discipline. * Excellent Communication skills * Professional and confident communicator who is able to build effective relationships with external facing stakeholders i.e., brokers/clients. * Good analytical skills * IT literate * Willingness to attend external and internal training courses. * Comfortable with traveling to different offices, which may include international travel. * Out of hours meetings i.e., networking/relationship building may be necessary. **What We Offer** The Tokio Marine HCC Group of Companies offers a competitive salary and employee benefit package. We are a successful, dynamic organization experiencing rapid growth and are seeking energetic and confident individuals to join our team of professionals. The Tokio Marine HCC Group of companies is an equal opportunity employer. Please visit www.tmhcc.com for more information about our companies. \#LI\-HJ1

title: Growth Account Executive
description: At ClickUp, we're building the future of work: the first truly converged AI workspace unifying tasks, docs, chat, calendar, and enterprise search, all supercharged by context\-driven AI. We are an AI\-native company. Every team member is expected to leverage AI daily, and we evaluate AI fluency as part of our hiring process. Join us and help redefine what's possible. 🚀 We’re looking for an Growth Account Executive who has a consistent track record of exceeding quota via net new account acquisition, plus some account management experience with expansion and renewals. You should have experience with consultative sales processes, selling SaaS (ideally productivity solutions), and working with mid\-size to large companies, across a variety of industries. You are a solution seller by nature, training, and experience, who is genuinely curious, and confident speaking with customers. And, you’re a persuasive self\-starter, a good listener, while having a high degree of personal accountability in communicating cross\-functionally and maintaining a healthy sales pipeline. Since a majority of your time will be spent on emails, calls, and web conferences with customers, we expect an infectiously positive attitude when prospecting, presenting, and closing! We are not interested in aggressive or annoying sales tactics \- we believe the product is the real reason for converting. Our secret sauce should be framed around how we can ensure people see the value of the solution we provide. If you’re ready for the ride of a lifetime into the billion\-dollar unicorn club, please, by all means, click the apply button! **The Role:** * Work all new business leads for Growth (100\-300 employee companies) * Achieve and consistently exceed pipeline and sales targets by converting free trials, marketing\-qualified leads, or demo requests into paid customers. * Master the product and be able to field customer service and product questions from your leads. * Perform customer presentations and demos via online web conferencing tools. * Work with the Growth and Marketing teams to optimize lead generation and sales conversion tools. * Identify customer segments and new opportunities to improve the quality of the sales pipeline. **Qualifications:** * 2\-4 years of relevant sales experience, preferably in all sales cycles, from converting net new prospects to growing and retaining existing customers. * 2\-4 years of SaaS sales cycle experience. * Excellent written, verbal, and presentation skills. * Process\-driven with diligent attention to detail. * Experience with completing and leading Requests for Information or Requests for Quotes. * Ability to work from our Dublin, Ireland office 3x per week. **Desirable:** * Experience with project management software or other productivity tools. * Experience with e\-commerce signup flows and field sales. * Experience with Slack, Front, Salesforce, and Outreach. * Some exposure to Customer Service and Technical Support processes, and sending responses quickly. * You prefer building rapport with customers over annoying, aggressive sales tactics. Unsure if you meet all the qualifications of this job description but are deeply excited about the role? We hire based on ambition, grit, and a passion for improving the way people work. If you think ClickUp is the company for you, we encourage you to apply! At ClickUp, we assess every candidate based on the potential impact they can have. We hire the best people for the job and support each person’s journey to build their boldest career. **Equal Opportunity Employer** *ClickUp is an Equal Opportunity Employer, and qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, or national origin.* **Privacy Notice** *ClickUp collects and processes personal data in accordance with applicable data protection laws. You can find further details by viewing our* *Global Candidate Privacy Notice.* *If you are a Philippine Job Applicant, please also see our* *Philippine Data Privacy Notice* *for further details.* **Visa Sponsorship** *Please note we are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment visa for roles outside of engineering and product at this time. Sponsorship for engineering and product roles is not guaranteed, but is instead based on the business needs for that specific role at that time. Please reach out to the recruiter with any questions.* **Fraud Alert** *ClickUp Talent Acquisition will only initiate contact via an **@*** ***clickup.com*** *email or through our official careers portal on* *clickup.com* *. We will never request fees, payments, or sensitive personal information. Please disregard any offers received outside these channels and report them to support@clickup.com.* **AI Processing Notice** *ClickUp may use artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies to help review and screen candidates' employment applications against role\-related criteria. These tools support, but do not replace, human decision‑making. If you have questions or need an accommodation in the recruitment process, please contact us at AskPeople@ClickUp.com.*

title: Haemovigilance Officer
description: **Haemovigilance Officer Job Description** **Job Title:** Haemovigilance Officer **Hours:** Part Time 24 hrs per Week (Job Sharing ) **Location:** Pathology Laboratory **Reporting Arrangements** This post will work closely on a day\-to\-day basis with the Chief / Senior Medical Scientist \& Medical Scientists in Blood Transfusion and will report to the Consultant Haematologist and Laboratory Manager. The Haemovigilance Officer is professionally accountable to the Director of Nursing for matters relating to nursing practice. **Key Working Relationships:** The post holder will work closely with: The Laboratory Manager The Consultant Haematologist Senior Medical Scientist \& Medical Scientists working in Blood Transfusion The Laboratory Quality Manager The Director of Nursing for all matters relating to nursing practice **Purpose of the Role** The post holder will have responsibility to ensure that Beacon Hospital meets all national and international standards relating to safe transfusion practice. To maintain highest standards of transfusion safety within Beacon hospital based on best practice guidelines **Principal Duties and Responsibilities** The key responsibilities and duties of the Haemovigilance Officer in Beacon Hospital have been divided into five essential categories 1\. Clinical Knowledge 2\. Quality , Change management ,\& Audit 3\. Preparing , Delivering and Evaluating the Education programme 4\. Information Technology 5\. Personal Qualities **Clinical Knowledge** Must possess specially focused knowledge and skills in transfusion practice at a higher level than other clinical staff. Identifies and seeks to reduce wastage of blood / blood components Identifies and promotes appropriate transfusion alternatives Develops and manages systems for identifying , managing, reporting and closing off serious adverse events / reactions and near\-misses \& non\-conformances Provides leadership in clinical practice \& acts as a resource for clinical staff Generates and contributes to the development \& continual updating of clinical policies and procedures Seeks to advocate on behalf of patients and families to participate in decisions about their transfusion requirements **Quality , Risk \& Audit** To initiate , support and implement quality improvement initiatives To work closely with the laboratory quality officer to ensure legislative and regulatory requirements relating to Haemovigilance are met Ensure all Haemovigilance audits are completed as per annual laboratory audit schedule Uses audit results to identify areas for improvement Uses change management skills to develop and quality Haemovigilance service To carry out root cause analysis following adverse events / non\-conformances and recommend corrective/ preventative actions to improve transfusion safety To assist transfusion staff in post transfusion infection lookbacks and product recalls To investigate all reports of transfusion reactions / events and report to consultant haematologist / Hospital Transfusion Committee (HTC) To attend internal Quality meetings and collate monthly / quarterly key performance indictors (KPI) To ensure the hospital achieves 100% traceability for blood and Blood components **Preparing , Delivering \& Evaluating a Haemovigilance Education Programme** Develops \& delivers education programme for all staff involved in transfusion practice Evaluates effectiveness of training provided Establishes \& maintains systems for managing training records for all Haemovigilance training provided Identifies own continuing professional development needs and engages appropriately **Information Technology** Proficiency in IT skills relevant to the role Familiarity with electronic blood management systems (Blood Track or equivalent) **Personnel Qualities** Ability to work independently and as part of a wider multidisciplinary team Excellent communication / negotiating skills \& ability to liaise with individuals / groups at all grades both internally \& externally Has strong results focus and ability to achieve results through collaborative working Has a professional and dedicated attitude to the role Is an effective leader and positive driver for change Demonstrates commitment to further education relevant to the role where appropriate Is committed to building a professional network to remain up\-to\-date with current Haemovigilance issues Demonstrates a strong willingness and ability to operate in the flexible manner that is essential for the effective delivery of the role. **Essential / Desirable Criteria** **Clinical Nurse Manager Grade 2 or Senior Medical Scientist** **Essential Qualifications / Experience** Be a registered Nurse or CORU registered Medical Scientist Have a minimum of 5 yrs post registration experience Hold a post graduate qualification relevant to the role (Haemovigilance / Risk Management / Blood Transfusion / Haematology) Possess excellent organisational , written, interpersonal communication skills Proficient in IT skills (Word / Excel /PowerPoint ) **Desirable Qualifications / Experience** Previous Haemovigilance experience Experience in education / training Experience in root cause analysis / risk management / audit This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the hospital and the post holder develop this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the hospital'

title: Health And Safety Officer
description: **Health and Safety Officer (Construction)** One of Ireland’s top construction companies is looking for a Health and Safety Officer with a minimum of 4 years' experience to join their team on a large residential project in South Dublin (Apartments). The successful candidate will report to the EHS Manager and will have ample opportunities for career progression and upskilling due to the expansion of the company. We are particularly interested in candidates with previous experience working on similar types of construction projects for a Main Contractor. **Role:** * Implementing and monitoring site\-specific Health \& Safety plans. * Keeping detailed records of inspection results and implementing corrective actions as necessary. * Organising, attending, and chairing safety meetings to ensure clear communication and alignment on safety practices. * Providing ongoing Health \& Safety training and support to staff and subcontractors. * Reporting and investigating accidents, incidents, and near misses, ensuring appropriate follow\-up and preventative measures. * Ensuring all equipment is safe to operate, working closely with site managers and maintenance teams. * Maintaining accurate records of all safety documentation, inspections, and audits. * Ensuring adherence to Health \& Safety legislation and company policies in accordance with Health \& Safety Authority requirements **Requirements:** * Relevant qualification in Health and Safety * Minimum 3 years’ experience working on building projects in Ireland or the UK * Full, clean driving license * Strong IT skills, particularly in Microsoft Office and Excel * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills **Package:** * Salary: €70k\-€80k * Pension and benefits package * Excellent career development opportunities * Company vehicle or car allowance If you’re a Health and Safety Officer considering a new role, please send your CV or contact Declan Hasson for a confidential discussion. GPC will not forward any CVs to clients without first discussing with you and getting your consent. GPC is a Dublin\-based recruitment consultancy with over 27 years of experience in recruiting construction professionals in Ireland and internationally

title: Health Safety Environment Advisor
description: At Apleona, we are always on the lookout for dynamic and talented people to join our team, as we currently have both a full\-time and part\-time role as a Mobile HSE Advisor **Full Time Monday to Friday 09:00 \- 17:00 Mobile Dublin and surrounding areas** **Part\-time 2 days a week, Dublin City Centr** e The appointed mobile HSE Advisor role will be responsible for providing a professional health and safety service to all levels of staff working within client organisations. **Main duties and responsibilities** * Developing and administering procedures (including safety statement, risk assessments and safety\-related protocols, environmental aspects register, etc) to ensure compliance with Health and Safety legislative requirements and corporate Environmental and Health \& Safety policy; * Assist in the maintenance and development of ISO45001, ISO14001 and ISO9001 as part of the Integrated Management system * Conducting and reviewing risk assessments and ensuring measures to control risks are implemented and monitored both for Apleona Ireland activities and as part of EH\&S client projects; title: IT Server & Network Hardware Support Technician

Infrastructure Supported * Network switches and chassis * Top of Rack (ToR) switches * Firewalls and load balancers (under engineering direction) * Wi\-Fi controllers and access points * Server hardware platforms including HPE, Dell, and Supermicro * Out\-of\-band management systems including iLO, iDRAC, and IPMI Operational Responsibilities * Manage incidents and service requests through ServiceNow, Email, and Microsoft Teams. * Perform site walkthroughs and job validation checks. * Receive, stage, and manage hardware inventory. * Support equipment logistics and secure movement within facilities. * Adhere to all operational, security, safety, and compliance procedures. **Required Skills \& Experience** **Essential** * 1\-2 years' experience in a similar IT Infrastructure Support role. * Experience supporting data centre server and network hardware. * Understanding of Windows and Linux environments. * Knowledge of network infrastructure and networking concepts. * Network Administration experience. * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Strong organisational and documentation skills. * Ability to work independently with minimal supervision. * Ability to adapt quickly and learn new technologies. * Strong team\-working and interpersonal skills. * Manual handling experience. * IT\-related certification in Computer Science, Information Systems, Electronics, Networking, or similar discipline. **Desirable** Experience with: * Windows Server * Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) * Storage platforms such as 3PAR, PowerMax, NetApp, and Object Storage * Knowledge of scripting languages such as: * PowerShell * Bash * Python * Perl * DOS scripting **Apply Now** If you have experience supporting network and server infrastructure and are looking for an exciting opportunity within a mission\-critical enterprise environment, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Maintenance Technician
description: **Facility Maintenance Technician** **About Your New Employer** * Join a rapidly expanding player in the Irish food production sector, recognized for their commitment to quality and operational excellence. * Be part of an organization with a long\-standing presence in the industry, a reputation for innovation, and a focus on high standards and employee well\-being. * Thrive in an engaging, growth\-oriented workplace where your technical and operational input truly matters! **About Your New Job** * As the Facility Maintenance Technician, you will be responsible for the smooth functioning and general upkeep of all manufacturing areas and support facilities, rapidly resolving technical issues as they arise. * You will develop procedures to make operations more efficient, emphasizing inventory controls, reducing waste, and fine\-tuning workflow processes. * Work closely with management to align maintenance priorities with changing business needs and offer strategic support when required. * Oversee and optimize spending related to facilities and equipment, always seeking opportunities for cost improvement. * Design and update plans for emergency incidents, ensuring safety standards are maintained for everyone onsite. * Take charge of regular and preventative engineering work, including troubleshooting and repairing production machinery. * Identify technical issues using PLC diagnostics, driving continuous improvements on the factory floor. **What Skills You Need** * At least 3 years working in a similar role within manufacturing, ideally in a regulated, high\-output environment. * Fully qualified Electrician (National Craft Senior Trades or similar certification required). * Background in Lean Six Sigma settings advantageous. * Excellent analytical, communication, and organizational skills. * Residency and work eligibility for full\-time roles in Ireland. * Understanding of relevant industry standards and regulations considered a plus. **What's on Offer** * Attractive compensation package that meets or exceeds industry norms. * Opportunities to rise within an evolving team and access to ongoing skills development. * A collaborative company culture committed to employee success and supportive management. **What's Next** Excited to step up in your maintenance career? Click "Apply Now" to start your application or contact us directly to learn more. Not the exact match for you? Connect with us anyway \-there are always engineering and manufacturing roles available to discuss.

title: Manufacturing Engineer
description: Our client is a leading global medical device manufacturer based in Dublin, dedicated to the design and manufacture of injectable drug delivery and containment systems. They operate to the highest quality and regulatory standards within an FDA\-regulated, ISO 13485 environment. They are currently seeking a **Process Engineer** to join their Dublin site on an initial 6\-month contract, supporting a critical preventive maintenance, calibration and FDA audit\-readiness initiative. This is an on\-site role with an immediate start. **Responsibilities** * Develop and document preventive maintenance procedures and processes for manufacturing equipment and utilities * Develop and standardise calibration procedures for critical process and utility instrumentation * Review existing maintenance and calibration documentation, identify gaps, and define what is required to bring the site to FDA audit readiness * Build the supporting procedures, justifications and documentation required for inspection readiness * Work cross\-functionally with Engineering, Maintenance, Quality and Manufacturing teams to embed compliant, audit\-ready processes * Support continuous improvement of maintenance reliability and compliance programmes **Experience** * Engineering background (Mechanical, Manufacturing, Process or related discipline) with experience that has moved into process development around preventive maintenance and calibration * Minimum 4 years' experience in an FDA\-regulated manufacturing environment (medical device, pharmaceutical or biotechnology) * Demonstrated experience developing PM and calibration procedures and documentation * Experience supporting FDA audit readiness, including reviewing current practices, identifying gaps and preparing justifications * Strong technical writing and documentation skills, with the ability to build and structure compliant processes * Knowledge of GMP and FDA / ISO 13485 requirements; CMMS experience (e.g. SAP PM) an advantage **To learn more about this role apply online or contact Lewis Murray @lewis.murray@hrmrecruit.com for a confidential discussion.**

title: Marketplace Content & SEO Executive
description: **Join Luzern eCommerce: Powering the World’s Most Ambitious Brands** Step into the world of limitless possibilities with Luzern eCommerce, where we’ve been redefining online retail since 2003\. We partner with global icons like On Running, Wella, Nestlé, Mattel, Panasonic, HTC, and Philips to accelerate growth, maximize margins, and deliver exceptional customer experiences across Amazon, top marketplaces, branded webstores, and social platforms. Backed by significant investment, we’re on a mission to dominate the global eCommerce space. With our award\-winning team, cutting\-edge AI\-enabled Channel Optimizer platform, and bold strategies, we’re not just keeping up with the future of retail—we’re creating it. Be part of one of the fastest\-growing tech sectors, where you’ll innovate, collaborate, and thrive in a business that values your individuality and ambition. Join Luzern eCommerce and be part of the next big chapter in retail history! **About the Role** We are looking for a creative and commercially\-minded eCommerce Content \& SEO Executive to join the team. The right candidate will have a strong instinct for compelling product copy and a deep understanding of what makes content perform across eCommerce and D2C channels. At the core of this role is SEO. You will lead our approach to content optimisation across marketplace PDPs (Amazon and other key marketplaces) and support DTC product pages, driving visibility, click\-through and conversion through both creative content and a sharp understanding of how algorithms and search behaviour are evolving. This is not a support role; we need someone who will proactively own and push our SEO content strategy forward. Tasks **What You’ll Be Doing** **SEO Strategy \& Innovation** * Lead the SEO content strategy across marketplace listings (Amazon\-first) and DTC product pages, ensuring content is continuously optimised for visibility and conversion * Stay ahead of changes to marketplace and search engine algorithms, proactively identifying opportunities and adapting our approach before competitors do * Drive SEO innovation — testing new content formats, keyword strategies, and structural approaches to PDPs to improve ranking and performance * Conduct regular keyword research and competitor analysis to inform content decisions across all channels * Monitor SEO performance using relevant tools and translate data into clear, actionable improvements * Build and maintain a working knowledge of marketplace\-specific ranking factors **SEO Copywriting** * Write high\-quality, conversion\-focused product descriptions, titles, and bullet points optimised for both search and the customer * Adapt tone and structure for different channels — marketplace listings, DTC PDPs, and campaign content — while maintaining brand consistency * Balance SEO requirements with genuine creative quality, producing copy that ranks and reads well * Develop a strong understanding of the Client’s brand, product ranges and customers to write with authority and relevance * Comfortable with testing content optimisations to understand which performs better, based on the data **Content Optismation \& Management** * Own the end\-to\-end quality of product detail pages across marketplaces and DTC, from copy and imagery through to structured data and enhanced content (A\+ Content, Brand Stores, etc.) * Conduct regular audits of all Marketplace content elements, for both existing and prosprospective clients, to build out best in class practices and develop content strategies. * Upload and update product listings, images, and copy across eCommerce platforms, ensuring accuracy and brand alignment at all times * Manage content updates across key marketplace accounts, maintaining high standards of data quality and presentation **Reporting \& Collaboration** * Track and report on content and SEO performance, clearly communicating results and recommendations to the wider team * Liaise with internal teams and clients to gather content briefs, product information, and campaign requirements * Maintain content trackers, calendars, and documentation to keep projects on track * Support general eCommerce admin tasks where required Requirements **What You Bring to the Role** **Essential** * Amazon specific knowledge on SEO and best in class practices in relation to content creation. * An understanding of agentic commerce and how content must be optimised for AI search. * Proven creative copywriting ability with a portfolio or examples of eCommerce or product content * Solid, working knowledge of SEO, not just the basics, but an understanding of how to apply it strategically in an eCommerce context * Experience optimising marketplace PDPs, ideally Amazon, with an understanding of how marketplace search differs from Google * Comfortable working with CMS platforms and eCommerce systems * Ability to analyse performance data and use it to make informed content decisions * Strong attention to detail — accuracy in product information and copy is non\-negotiable * Highly organised, able to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines independently **Desirable** * Experience using SEO tools such as Helium 10, Jungle Scout or similar * Familiarity with Amazon Seller Central or Vendor Central and enhanced content formats (A\+ Content, Brand Stores) * Experience with product information management (PIM) systems * Understanding of DTC eCommerce platforms (Shopify, Magento, or similar) * Basic image editing ability (Figma, Canva, or similar) * Experience working with clients or across multiple brands **Personal Attributes** * Creative first — you care about the quality of the words as much as the rankings they achieve * Proactive and curious about the SEO landscape — you follow industry changes and bring ideas to the table without being asked * Commercially aware — you understand that great content has to drive results, not just look good * Detail\-oriented and methodical without losing sight of the bigger picture * A strong communicator who can explain SEO decisions clearly to non\-specialists * Collaborative team player who can also work independently and manage their own time * Genuine interest in eCommerce, retail, and digital marketing Benefits **What We Offer** * **Competitive Compensation:** Attractive salary package with benefits. * **Work\-Life Balance:** Competitive annual leave and flexible working arrangements. * **Growth Opportunities** : Access to learning and development programs to enhance your skills. * **Culture and Environment** : A fun, inclusive, and collaborative workplace. * **Wellbeing Support:** Access to an Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) * **Quarterly Social Events:** Company\-sponsored quarterly social events designed to bring teams together, celebrate success, and strengthen connections across the business. We are committed to equal opportunities and welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates. If you require any reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know.

title: Multi-Skilled Technician
description: **About The Role** As a CBRE Multi\-skilled Technician, you will perform ongoing preventive maintenance and repairs on a mechanical, electrical, and other building system. This job is part of the Engineering and Technical Services job function. They are responsible for providing support, preventive maintenance, and repairs on equipment and systems. **What You’ll Do** * Conduct routine maintenance inspections, diagnose potential problems, and make repairs. * Assist with installation and modification of building equipment and systems. * Review assigned work orders and estimate the time and materials needed to complete repairs. Work with available systems to manage and track completion. * Support energy management by ensuring all building systems are operating efficiently. * Inspect new or existing installations for compliance with building codes and safety regulations. Report violations to Management. * Use existing procedures to solve straightforward problems while having a limited opportunity to exercise discretion. * Impact through clearly defined duties, methods, and tasks are described in detail. * Deliver own output by following defined procedures and processes under close supervision and guidance. **What You’ll Need:** * High School Diploma, GED, or trade school diploma with 1\-2 years of job\-related experience. In lieu of a diploma, a combination of experience and education will be considered. * Meet the physical requirements of this role including stooping, standing, walking, climbing stairs/ladders, and the ability to lift/carry heavy loads of 50 lbs. or more. * Ability to follow basic work routines and standards in the application of work. * Communication skills to exchange straightforward information. * Working knowledge of Microsoft Office products. Examples include Word, Excel, Outlook, etc. * Strong organizational skills with an inquisitive mindset.

title: National Coach Developer
description: **Job Title:** National Coach Developer **Location:** FAI HQ, Abbottstown or Regional **Direct Reporting Line:** Coach Development Manager **Duration:** Permanent / Full\-Time **Work Model:** Hybrid **Introduction** The Football Association of Ireland (FAI) is the national governing body for football in Ireland, driving the growth, promotion, and development of the game at all levels. We are passionate about inspiring participation, supporting talent, and celebrating the power of football to unite communities across the country. With a proud history and an ambitious vision for the future, the FAI is committed to fostering excellence on and off the pitch, ensuring the game is inclusive, accessible, and a source of national pride. **Purpose of the Role** The **National Coach Developer** will lead the delivery and development of the FAI’s Coach Development programmes, supporting coaches from entry\-level to UEFA licence pathways. The role will create and deliver high\-quality formal and informal learning opportunities, enhance coach skills and knowledge, and contribute to the professional growth of coaches across Ireland. **Key Responsibilities** Coach Development Pathways * Deliver formal coaching courses crossing the full Coach Development Pathway, from entry\-level to UEFA qualifications. * Facilitate engaging, inclusive, and high\-quality learning environments in both classroom and on\-pitch settings. * Provide ongoing mentoring, observation, and feedback to support coaches’ development beyond formal courses. * Develop and maintain informal learning initiatives, including communities of practice, workshops, peer learning, and online forums. * Contribute to the design and enhancement of coach development materials, resources, and assessment processes. Collaboration \& Stakeholder Engagement * Represent the FAI at regional and national events, promoting coaching excellence and development programmes. * Collaborate with Regional FAI staff, tutors, mentors, and player development programmes to ensure alignment across pathways. * Build partnerships with educational institutions. **Person Specification** **Experience and Qualifications** Essential: * UEFA B Licence. * Track record in designing, delivering, and evaluating coach development programmes. * Significant coaching and/or coach development experience. * Experience mentoring or supporting coaches in formal and informal settings. * Excellent communication, facilitation, and relationship\-management skills. * Strong administrative skills and familiarity with relevant IT systems. * Experience managing projects and working collaboratively. * Experience delivering learner\-centred, reality\-based coaching environments. * Strong understanding of the UEFA Coaching Convention and FAI Coach Education framework. * Full Clean Driving licence. Desirable: * UEFA A Licence or higher * Experience as a Coach Developer, Tutor, or Mentor in a recognised association. * QQI Coach Developer Level 7\. * Formal training or qualification in coach education, sports coaching, or adult learning. * Experience using digital learning tools and blended education approaches. * Knowledge of the Irish football structure and grassroots\-to\-elite pathways. **Skills \& Professional Knowledge** * A positive and proactive team player with excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Passionate about supporting coach and player development with a reflective, learner\-centered approach. * Innovative and open to new approaches and technologies in coach education. * Committed to inclusivity, diversity, and excellence in football coaching. * Passionate about helping others grow and succeed in coaching. * Reflective, empathetic, and learner\-centred in approach. * Innovative and open to new ideas and technologies in education. * Excellent collaborator with a team\-focused mindset. * Committed to inclusivity, diversity, and excellence in Irish football. **Core Competencies\-Aligned with Cultural Values** ***(Progress/Passion/Togetherness*****)** **Innovation \& Continuous Improvement** ***(Progress)*** Thinks creatively and with an open mind to identify new opportunities for growth and improvement. Embraces change positively and contribute to building a better future for Irish football. **Drive \& Commitment** ***(Passion*****)** Demonstrates energy, enthusiasm, and pride in everything they do. Takes initiative, delivers results, and strives to make a meaningful impact on the game and the organisation. **Collaboration \& Teamwork** ***(Togetherness)*** Works effectively and respectfully with others to achieve shared goals. Supports, challenges, and motivates colleagues to perform at their best, recognising that success is achieved together. **Communication \& Influence** ***(Togetherness / Passion)*** Communicates clearly, openly, and respectfully with colleagues and stakeholders. Builds positive relationships, inspires trust, and represents the FAI with professionalism and integrity. **Accountability \& professional Excellence (*****Progress / Passion*****)** Takes ownership of performance and upholds high standards in all tasks. Acts responsibly, follows through on commitments, and contributes to the FAI’s vision of continuous progress and excellence. **Our Benefits** We are proud of the range of benefits we can provide: * Two premium level tickets to each home international game * Sick pay scheme and family friendly leave * Opportunities to work at international matches * Company pension * Life assurance * Bike to work scheme * Free onsite parking * Employee assistance programs * Hybrid working arrangements and flexible hours working arrangements * Discounted access to the National Aquatic Centre and gym complex **Application Details** Closing date for receipt of applications is close of business on **Friday 10th July 2026\.** Shortlisting and interviewing process will take place thereafter. **The Football Association of Ireland is an equal opportunities employer and promotes Equity, Diversity \& Inclusion (ED\&I).**

title: OEM Business & Market Development – Drive Products
description: At **ABB** , we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world. **This position reports to:** Local Division Manager \_\_ In this role you will be responsible for driving the sales strategy and G2M model for sales in the Drive Products and IEC LV Induction motors market within Irelands OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturers), machine building and industrial market sector, aligned with the division strategy. This includes low voltage drives, PLC \& HMI, and IEC low voltage motors offering to different types of OEMs and machine builders across different segments and applications within the Irish and Northern Irish market. Delivers both qualitative and quantitative sales targets to ensure sustainable order growth, revenues, profitability, market share, and customer satisfaction. Responsible to identify and developing new OEM sales opportunities. **Our Team Dynamics** Our teams support each other, collaborate, and never stop learning. Everyone brings something unique, and together we push ideas forward to solve real problems. Being part of our team means your work matters \- because the progress we make here creates real impact out there. **What’s in it for you?** We want you to bring your full self to work—your ideas, your energy, your ambition. You’ll have the tools and freedom to grow your skills, shape your path, and take on challenges that matter. Here, your work creates impact you can see and feel, every day. **Your Role And Responsibilities** In this role, you’ll help run what runs the world, by taking on meaningful work that drives real impact. * Work model: Office based with travel and flexibility. * Review the local market, identify and execute predefined market strategies. * Drive volume, mix, and profitability targets for selected customers across the division. * Build and maintain strategic customer relationships to understand their needs and position ABB's drive product solutions. * Leverage cross\-division synergies to enhance customer value and ensure a seamless sales experience. * Develop short\-to\-medium term sales plans using ABB tools. * Conduct regular pipeline reviews and propose recovery actions for any shortfalls. * Prepare competitive offers in coordination with the bid and proposal team, ensuring technical and financial accuracy. * Validate pricing, trading conditions, and margins on all proposals. * Communicate delivery timelines, payment terms, and contractual conditions aligned with ABB strategy. * Develop and communicate value propositions through marketing activities. * Manage sales administration procedures, commercial decisions, and support project management as needed. **Qualifications For The Role** * Degree in Automation, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related field * Marketing / Business qualification or some time working in a similar business environment with a track record of building customer relationships considered a bonus * Previous experience in engaging business to position ABB as a valued solutions provider vrs competitors * Results\-oriented mindset combined with the engineering and business acumen * Strategic in approach and confidence to take initiative, and turn ideas into action * Strong communication and collaboration skills with the ability to build trust across stakeholders * Genuine drive to learn, grow, and continuously improve, both personally and as a sales individual **Benefits** ABB offers a full range of benefits to help you thrive at work and beyond. * Competitive Salary * Private Medical Allowance for Yourself and Family * Competitive Contributory Pension Scheme * Employee Share Acquisition Plan * Internal Mobility Opportunities, Mentorships \& Access to Projects Globally * Employee Assistance Programme * Generous Employee Referral Scheme * 25 Days Annual Leave Plus Bank Holidays * Company Car * Flexible Working * Generous Bonus Scheme **More About Us** ABB's Drive Products and IEC Low Voltage Motors division delivers motors, integrated motors, drives, and automation solutions for OEMs and machine builders globally. Our comprehensive portfolio combines ultra premium\-efficiency IEC Low Voltage Motors, variable frequency drives, AC500 PLCs, and HMIs to create optimised automation packages that reduce costs, reduce downtime, improve process throughput, and lower operational carbon footprint. With electric motors consuming 45% of global electricity and high\-efficiency systems cutting consumption by 10%, our integrated solutions help OEMs across blue\-chip manufacturing, water, food \& beverage, mining, and data centers build smarter, more efficient machines and operations. Join us. Be part of the team where progress happens, industries transform, and your work shapes the world. Run What Runs the World. \#ABBCareers Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences. Ready to make an impact? Apply today or visit **https://www.abb.com** to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

title: Painter & Decorator -Drogheda- Dundalk-Dunleer
description: **Professional Painter \& Decorator** **Location:** Our base is in Dundalk, Co. Louth, where we have two sites, but we also have projects in Drogheda and Dunleer **Experience Required:** 5\+ Years as Professional Paintiner \& Decorator with a painting contractor \- No DIY painters please **Employment Type:** Full\-Time (39 hours/week) **About the Role:** We are seeking a skilled and reliable **Painter \& Decorator** with over 5 years of professional experience to join our growing team. You’ll be working on a variety of projects, including 2 major new housing developments in Dundalk with 120 units, a new housing development in Drogheda with 97 units and a new housing development in Dunleer with 70 units scheduled for completion over the next 6 \- 12 months, along with ongoing residential and commercial projects throughout the North East. **Key Requirements:** * Minimum 5 years of professional painting \& decorating experience * Must hold a valid **Safe Pass** and **Manual Handling Certificate** * Punctual, dependable, and able to work as part of a team **Pay \& Benefits:** * **€21/hour** – for experienced painters (5\+ years, without formal qualifications) * **€23/hour** – for time\-served, qualified painters (5\+ years with papers) * **€5 daily tea allowance** * **39 hours per week** * **20 days paid annual leave** * **12 paid bank holidays** * **Monthly performance bonus** for attendnace and meeting production targets * **Christmas bonus** based on service length and productivity * **Company pension, sick and death in service benefit schemes** **Interested?** Join a company that values skill, reliability, and teamwork—where your work is recognised and rewarded. title: Part-time Sales Associate
description: **Love a job where every day looks a little different?** Our newest store just opened in Jervis. We're looking for someone who's great with people and even better with details. **MemoryBlock Ireland is hiring seasonal part\-time staff for our new Jervis Centre store in Dublin City.** You'll be the face of the brand in\-store: helping customers turn their favourite photos into something they'll keep for life, made right there in front of them. **What you'll be doing:** * Engaging with customers, understanding what they're looking for, and recommending the right product for the moment * Handling cash and our POS system, entering orders accurately * Keeping displays, shelves, and the store itself looking sharp and on\-brand * Juggling multiple customer requests at once and hitting order turnaround times * Comfortable with email, online order systems, and basic digital photo handling (we'll train you on our tools) * Restocking and light physical tasks \- you'll be on your feet for parts of the shift * Flexible across weekends and peak shopping periods **What we're looking for:** Retail, gifting, or customer\-facing experience is a bonus but not essential. What matters more is that you're a quick learner, comfortable talking to people, and good under pressure during busy moments. Photoshop knowledge is a nice plus. **You'll also need to be flexible to rotate across our stores when needed, including Blanchardstown, Nutgrove, and Jervis.** This is a seasonal part\-time position that has a chance of extension if you show exceptional skills and ability and if the open position presents itself. **This is a seasonal part\-time position based at our Jervis Centre store.** Think this sounds like you? Apply now and tell us why. Get in touch today \- Email us at karmika@memoryblock.ie

title: Partner Relationship Representative
description: **Please note this is role is located from our Sandyford, Dublin Office.** Do you have a background with POS / EPOS solutions or Integrated payments solutions, creating new partnerships relationships and growing merchant activations / revenue growth? viva.com, a trailblazer in innovative payment services across Europe, is searching for talented and motivated Partner Relationship Representative to take ownership of the partnership program and play a pivotal role in shaping the future of payment solutions in the Ireland. **About Viva.com** Viva.com is the 1st Tech Bank in Europe for Businesses revolutionizing omnichannel payment acceptance, offering card issuing, loans, and deposit accounts to businesses across Europe. Licensed by the Bank of Greece, it operates under the EU FOS regime in 22 EU countries, delivering seamless financial services. Viva.com is the first to pioneer and holds the top position in the Tap on Any Device technology, enabling payments acceptance on any device or even without a device. Today Viva.com is leading the transition to All\-in\-One business solutions, powered by its full suite of financial services. To that end, reflecting its holistic approach to business needs, Viva.com has built a dynamic ecosystem featuring over 450 tech partner\-innovators in the software and hardware space. Connecting directly to all local payment schemes, alternative payment methods and payment systems across the continent, further strengthens Viva.com's value proposition. Viva.com envisions to provide businesses a value\-driven platform to accelerate the adoption of the latest technology. **Role Overview:** Indirect Team Representatives focus on supporting partners at a national level, ensuring effective onboarding, verification, and activation of new merchants. **Key Responsibilities:** * Assist partners in onboarding, verifying, and activating new merchants; * Identify and engage new prospective partners to promote company's collaboration framework; * Escalate partner requests by opening cases for issues beyond their resolution capacity; * Serve as the first point of contact for partners; * Drive production and growth within the Indirect Team; * Interest in AI\-powered tools and openness to working in AI\-enhanced environments **Requirements** * 2\+ years of experience in working cross\-functionally to enhance partner relationships and business impact; * Ability to identify both business potential and added value in technology\-driven commercial solutions; * Be a strong stakeholder with engagement skills and the ability to interact confidently with a wide variety of people at all levels; * Be able to work independently and as part of a team to deliver parallel projects; * Strong language skills with the ability to speak English at business level. **Benefits** 💸 Competitive compensation package based on experience; 🎯 Performance\-based bonus scheme tied to individual and team results; 🏥 Private health insurance coverage; 💻 Opportunity to work with the latest payments technology and help grow a highly innovative organisation.

title: Payroll Specialist
description: Moore is a leading Irish provider of accountancy and advisory services for smart, commercially ambitious enterprises seeking the highest standards in client service and expertise. We are currently seeking to recruit a **Payroll Specialist** to join our team. With excellent opportunities for career progression this is a position will be based at our Dun Laoghaire office. The role will be responsible for the accurate and timely processing of weekly and monthly payrolls in compliance with Irish Revenue requirements and company policies. The role includes payroll administration, statutory reporting, pension auto\-enrolment support, and payroll systems management using Sage Quantum and Thesaurus software. **Key Responsibilities:** * Process weekly and monthly payrolls accurately and within deadlines. * Administer payroll for salaried and hourly paid employees. * Ensure compliance with Irish payroll legislation and Revenue requirements. * Submit Revenue Payroll Notifications (RPNs) and payroll submissions through Revenue Online Service (ROS). * Manage Enhanced Reporting Requirements (ERR) submissions to Revenue. * Support pension auto\-enrolment administration and deductions. * Process statutory payments including sick pay, maternity, paternity, and parental leave. * Maintain accurate payroll records and employee data. * Reconcile payroll reports, deductions, PAYE, PRSI, USC, and pension contributions. * Prepare payroll journals and reports for finance and management teams. * Handle employee payroll queries in a professional and confidential manner. * Ensure compliance with GDPR and confidentiality standards. * Assist with payroll audits and year\-end payroll activities. * Liaise with HR, Finance, Revenue, pension providers, and external auditors as required. * Maintain and update payroll procedures and controls. * Use Sage Quantum and Thesaurus payroll software effectively for payroll processing and reporting. **Experience and Qualifications Required:** * Payroll qualification (IPASS or equivalent). * Previous experience processing Irish payrolls. * Knowledge of PAYE, PRSI, USC, and Irish payroll legislation. * Experience with Revenue payroll submissions and ROS. * Knowledge of Enhanced Reporting Requirements (ERR). * Experience using Sage Quantum and Thesaurus payroll software. * Experience processing weekly and monthly payrolls. * High level of accuracy and attention to detail. * Strong organisational and time management skills. * Good communication and interpersonal skills. * Proficient in Microsoft Excel and Microsoft Office. **What Moore Offers:** * Competitive salary and benefits package * A good work life\-balance * Hybrid working options * Career development and progression opportunities * In\-house training and CPD support * Mentoring system, continual assessment and feedback * A supportive and inclusive team culture * Active social club and wellness initiatives * Access to the Moore Global Network, with over 30,000 professionals in 110\+ countries **Who We Are looking For:** At Moore, we believe it is important to look at the overall candidate. We are passionate about diversity and inclusion, and we seek to attract talented individuals from a wide and diverse talent pool. Our business is made stronger by the different perspectives and experiences of our people and we feel it is important and in line with our values that we continue to build a workforce which is representative of our society as it is today. Candidates should be motived, collaborative, enthusiastic and driven in their approach to their career. Learn more about us at \- www.mooreireland.ie

title: Performance Tester

**performance** **testing** to help raise quality, reliability, and release confidence across distributed **Java\-based platforms.** You will design and evolve modern test automation approaches and frameworks, partner closely with engineers/SRE/product to shift quality left, and ensure we validate end\-to\-end customer journeys through a balanced strategy spanning functional, integration, verbal communication skillsâ€"able to explain technical issues succinctly, influence quality\-first engineering habits, and partner effectively across functions and geographies.* *Desirable / title: Post Production Support Specialist, Charles River Development, Assistant Vice President
description: ****What We Are Looking For**** The Post Production Support Specialist (PPSS) supports customers post go\-live of the Charles River Investment Management Solution (CRIMS) implementation. Their contributions include defining new client requirements, configuring and tuning the application to the client’s business and technical needs, training users, testing workflows and resolving issues. The Specialist will liaise with Charles River Technical Support and Product resources to troubleshoot application issues and to determine the optimal configuration. The ideal candidate will have at least 3 to 5 years of hands\-on experience gained in the financial industry working for a consultancy or software vendor. Experience leading the implementation or support of Front, Middle or Back Office Investment Management systems is highly valued. **Why this job is important to us** The team you will be joining is a part of Charles River Development (or CRD), that became a part of State Street in 2018\. CRD helps create enterprise investment management software solutions for large institutions in the areas of institutional investment, wealth management and hedge funds. Together we have created the first open front\-to\-back platform \- State Street Alpha, that was launched in 2019\. ****What You Will Be Responsible For**** * PPSS are expected to have strong industry knowledge across multiple asset classes. Additionally, PPSS team members are responsible for navigating the CRD/Alpha eco\-system and establishing relationships with the various internal supporting teams. * Analyze and evaluate requirements issues while providing product expertise and guidance throughout the post go\-live journey. * Educate customers on the methodologies and functionality provided by CRIMS. Advise on best practices regarding the implementation and operation of Charles River solutions and services. * Evaluate customer requirements to identify potential enhancements to Charles River solutions and services. Document and communicate product improvements as part of customer\-specific requirements or in response to trends in the marketplace. * Responsible for maintaining a client issues list and status reporting. * Train users on existing workflows * Gather requirements for new asset classes, security types and/or business line + Document requirements + Works with Professional Services team to assign dedicated resources to accomplish deliverables * Contribute to software quality by clearly communicating defects to Client Support and Product Management. Support the defect resolution process by providing clear descriptions of defects and validating the resulting fixes. Identify errors and mistakes in documentation and suggest corrections and improvements. ****What We Value**** The Post Go Live Production Support Specialists is expected to have experience working on implementing or supporting Front, Middle or Back Office Investment Management systems. * PPSS are expected to have strong industry knowledge across multiple asset classes. Additionally, PPSS team members are responsible for navigating the CRD/Alpha eco\-system and establishing relationships with the various internal supporting teams. * Minimum of 5 years of experience with the implementation and/or support of mission\-critical financial solutions. Experience in the implementation and/or support of a financial system within a bank, hedge fund or asset manager, or wealth management firm is especially valuable. * Hands\-on experience implementing or supporting investment management software, preferably an order management and compliance system is especially valuable. * Substantive in one or more of the following: fixed income, equity financial instruments. Understanding of investment management or wealth management workflows, derivatives, and FX instruments. * Technically savvy: understanding of multi\-tier technical architectures and relational database structures, familiarity with SQL, FIX and reporting tools. * A strong record of customer service. Post Go Live Support Specialists must understand customer needs and build effective relationships. The candidate must be able to convey plans, issues and concerns to clients and team members in a clear, logical and concise manner. They will need to also be able to influence key decision makers both internally and externally. * Must be customer driven, work within a team environment, and be focused on providing a high quality of service to the customer. * Strong time management skills including the ability to manage multiple projects in parallel. * Strong problem solving/analytical skills. ****Education \& Preferred Qualifications**** * Degree in a business or technical field such as Finance, Mathematical Finance, Economics, Engineering, or Computer Science. An advanced degree or industry certification such as the CFA is a strong plus. * 3 to 5\+ years of experience * Travel as required by the Customer ****About State Street**** **What we do.** State Street is one of the largest custodian banks, asset managers and asset intelligence companies in the world. From technology to product innovation we're making our mark on the financial services industry. For more than two centuries, we've been helping our clients safeguard and steward the investments of millions of people. We provide investment servicing, data \& analytics, investments research \& trading and investment management to institutional clients. **Work, Live and Grow.** We make all efforts to create a great work environment. Our benefits packages are competitive and comprehensive. Details vary in locations, but you may expect generous medical care, insurance and savings plans among other perks. You'll have access to flexible Work Program to help match your needs. And our wealth of development programs and educational support will help you reach your full potential. **Inclusion, Diversity, and Social Responsibility.** We truly believe our employees' diverse backgrounds, experience and perspective are a powerful contributor to creating an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive and reach their maximum potential while adding value to both our organization and our clients. We warmly welcome candidates of diverse origin, background, ability, age, sexual orientation, gender identity and personality. Another fundamental value at State Street is active engagement with our communities around the world, both as a partner and a leader. You will have tools to help balance your professional and personal life, paid volunteer days, matching gift program and access to employee networks that help you stay connected to what matters to you. State Street is an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. Company: Charles River Development Discover more at www.StateStreet.com/careers ****About State Street**** Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law. title: Power Systems Engineer
description: At **ABB**, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world. **This position reports to:** ELSE Cluster Manager North Europe \_\_ **Your role and responsibilities** In this role you will work as a power engineer on a range of exciting projects for global clients in a rewarding team environment, developing technical and solution development skills to develop into an industry\-recognized consulting engineer. The position would suit a practical and adaptable individual who wishes to work in a progressive, innovative electrical consultancy. We are seeking experienced Power Systems Engineers who are eager to deepen their technical expertise and contribute to complex engineering projects for global clients. This role is ideal for a practical, adaptable, and technically driven engineer who wants to grow into an industry\-recognized consulting specialist within a progressive and innovative electrical consultancy environment. The work model for the role is: \#hybrid, \#Li\-hybrid Driven by strong business growth and increasing customer demand, we are expanding our team and recruiting multiple professionals for this role across the listed countries. You will be mainly accountable for: * Providing technical input and leadership on a variety of complex projects. * Working closely with clients to understand their technical and business requirements and translating them into practical engineering solutions. * Ensuring project outputs meet client expectations, quality standards, timeframes, and budget requirements. * Conducting in\-depth power systems analysis using industry\-leading methodologies and software tools. * Preparing detailed technical and non\-technical reports, presenting clear recommendations and actionable insights. * Attending client meetings remotely, building trusted relationships and managing expectations through effective communication and responsiveness. * Tracking and reporting on project performance and client satisfaction. * Mentoring, coaching and developing more junior engineers. Online training to be delivered where applicable. **Typical projects include:** * Power system studies include load flow, short circuit, protection coordination and system resilience assessments. * Electrical safety and power quality studies, including arc flash, harmonics and earthing assessments. * Grid connection and renewable energy integration projects, covering stability, harmonics and compliance analyses. * Reliability assessments and failure investigations for critical electrical infrastructure. * Software\-based engineering solutions and emerging technology evaluations. * Supporting wind energy projects from design through installation and commissioning. **Qualifications for the role** * You are highly skilled in power system studies, including short circuit, protection coordination and arc flash analysis, and enjoy working on complex electrical infrastructure projects. * You have 3\+ years of experience in power systems engineering, applying technical expertise to deliver reliable, safe and efficient electrical solutions. * You possess advanced knowledge of industry\-leading power system analysis tools such as SKM, EasyPower, ETAP or similar. * You have proven experience in protection system design, settings development and coordination studies, with exposure to transformer and bus differential protection, REF and directional protection schemes. * You have experience performing electrical calculations and developing designs in accordance with relevant IEC standards. * Knowledge of power quality, harmonics and renewable energy integration studies would be an advantage. * You are passionate about solving technical challenges, collaborating with clients and stakeholders, and continuously expanding your expertise. * Degree in Electrical Engineering or a related field. * You are at ease communicating in English and can clearly present technical concepts to a wide range of audiences Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences. Ready to make an impact? Apply today or visit **https://www.abb.com** to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.
